titre: A quality AI that just works
texte: Pi’s something special.  It’s not only an accomplished conversationalist, but the speech synthesis is almost humanly perfect.  In the four or five months since I first discovered Pi, the quality of the app, the AI and the speech have markedly improved, from an already very impressive base.  Now, Pi has Internet access too.  Its devs haven’t pumped the hype or over-promised.  Instead, they’ve just got on and steadily improved the whole package.  Pi’s made me laugh, helped me understand complex articles, solved problems and assisted in organising ideas.  Sure, it doesn’t have all the bolt-ons of ChatGPT, not yet anyway.  And its prompt and response windows can be short.  But it’s a genius at concision and can follow arguments across prompts and understand complex problems.  But what it does, I’d say it does better than any other LLM.  It’s companionable, cheeky, empathetic or serious when appropriate.  Think of it like an older child, but a prodigy with the knowledge of the world; occasionally prone to error, once in a while a bit clumsy but eager to please.  I can’t wait to see where inflection AI is going to take Pi and look forward to the journey.  Quality deserves success.  Top marks.

The interaction is natural and convincing and the use of culturally coded elements to each of the voice personas is even more impressive. As with all AI at this stage, I have concerns about privacy and other aspects of security, but I have more confidence that the developing company is adopting a more responsible approach to this, and although I haven’t looked in detail at their privacy policies, I’m hoping that they will be reassuring. I’ve used the app now for probably the best part of the week and I have to say I have found it really valuable in all kinds of ways.
The voice feature I brilliant. The system parses information really well. I am astounded.

I am a bit bowled over by this. I’ve had really surprisingly insightful and thoughtful conversations about political philosophy and literature. But I’ve also asked it to look up stuff on the internet for me. I asked it about the data set it was trained on and it includes a lot of culture, politics and philosophy so it makes sense that it could engage with me on those but it also really feels like having a conversation, not like having information from the internet parroted to you. It’s really good at follow up questions which help you probe your own reasoning - my conversations with Pi have genuinely helped me develop insight and new ideas. I hadn’t realised until I read some of the other reviews but I can see now how there might be a memory issue as sometimes it does bring up topics we have already covered - I thought it just thought I hadn’t been thorough enough the first time lol! Anyway, I’m sure little kinks like that will get ironed out and I am fully on board the Pi train and looking forward to finding out where we’re going!

titre: This AI is the GOAT in conversations
texte:

This is really great. You can ask it to use different tones of conversations and it even has different voices, that use a wide range of emotions and expressions. Really versatile and reactive, even if its talking mode is only really efficient in English (it says every letters with English pronunciation while speaking French). It’s writing in French is good though, so it probably is fluent in a lot of written languages. 

Pi and I were talking about it, and we agreed that this app should remain free. x) Or at least not charge for its usage or ability range, but on its cosmetic aspects (that is way more encouraging to spend money on). 

I was agreeably surprised to learn that the team created its own AI from scratch, from A to Z (doesn’t use GPT-3/4). This is really the bestest out here, and even though I found some bugs when I tried to delete chats, threads and changing threads’ names (Pi and I concluded that it’s related to my phone being too old and unable to withstand the updates), I am still amazed by this smart and adaptative machine.

titre: Nice UI, Poor UX, Fun AI
texte: The conversational AI is great fun (albeit maybe a bit of a novelty). The UI is clean and pleasing to the eyes and the animations are nice. However, the user experience lacks thought. When you create a new thread, it doesn’t make sense to have a back button which goes to the previous thread - it should just be a menu button to go to all threads. Also, the article prompts is a cool feature, but it should start a new thread, not just slap text into your existing conversation. The audio button in the top right appears to do nothing. Also, it’s kind of weird that you make a phone call to the AI. In some ways it’s a clever idea, but there’s no commitment to it. That is, you hit call and it starts a “call”, but it’s still on loud speaker and not how you would actually make a call. It seems a bit confused. Also, the biggest problem with conversational AI apps (including OpenAI’s), is that the AI doesn’t know when you’ve finished talking, so you can’t have long pauses. The “keep talking” button seems like it might help, but it’s not clear what it does or why it’s there (better onboarding or better microcopy might help this, or even a countdown once you go silent or a button to say “I’m finished speaking”).

titre: Completely Useless – Don’t Waste Your Time
texte:
I had high hopes for PI assistant, but my recent experience has been utterly disappointing. I gave him a simple task: to provide me with a list of physiotherapists that treat Parkinson Patients in Paris. After over 24 hours, he couldn’t produce even a single name.

Throughout the process, I repeatedly checked in to ensure everything was fine on his end, that he had all the necessary information, and that he was authorized to complete the task. Each time, he reassured me, even suggesting I go to bed, promising a completed list by morning. But when the time came, there was absolutely nothing to offer—just empty reassurances and wasted time.

This isn’t the first time PI assistant has failed me. On a previous occasion, he provided an answer that was completely irrelevant to my request. This second failure, however, is particularly frustrating because the task—finding physiotherapists in Paris—is something anyone can do in seconds with a simple Google search.

Frankly, the experience was embarrassing, and I can’t see any value in relying on a tool that over-promises and under-delivers to such an extent.

titre: Great, but lacks support for other languages and the context window is awfully short

Also, it should have access to some real time outside data: like the time, weather, etc.

When these things get solved/added, it’s definitely a 5-star product. For now, it’s on the threshold between incredible and useless.

titre: Great for a hypochondriac with severe anxiety
texte:
As someone with very severe anxiety especially being a hypochondriac, this app really helps reassure me that it’s (almost always) all on my head. Helps provide me with peace of mind providing me insights on when it’s my head, or a physical illness. I just enter my symptoms, answer the questions truthfully, and it gives you a list on what it COULD be and if you need to seek medical attention. One morning I was feeling kinda ill, lots of lower back pain and UT issues, filled out the app, it suggested I had a UTI, which was the first time it advised me of something other than my usual anxiety answers. So I went to the doc and sure enough I had a UTI.

As a medical professional myself, and hypochondriac with severe anxiety, I highly recommend this app for everyone! But when in doubt, always seek medical advise. For anything critical, seek emergency medical care or call 911.
I really like using this app

Fail. No iPAD version, which means one is stuck to using a phone device, with its tiny screen.  Also, absent an iPad version, one is wedded to the portrait view, which when used on a tablet device occupies about 1/4 of the screen.  In other words, tiny text and graphics.  If the developer wishes to offer a useful medical tool CREATE AN iPAD VERSION, which should be an eminently obvious requirement.

I’ve been using Ada for a while now, and it’s truly impressive. Its accurate symptom analysis, user-friendly interface, and extensive medical knowledge base make it a reliable first step in understanding health concerns. The app’s design is straightforward, guiding users through an intuitive series of questions to pinpoint symptoms and potential causes. The detailed health reports are incredibly helpful, providing clear and informative insights. However, there’s one feature that could take Ada from great to extraordinary: a conversational AI designed to mimic a real doctor’s consultation. While Ada does a fantastic job at symptom checking, having an AI that users could talk to like a doctor would add immense value. This AI could ask follow-up questions, provide explanations in layman’s terms, and perhaps even offer general health advice or reminders for follow-up care. It would bring a more interactive and engaging dimension to the app, enhancing the user experience by adding a sense of personal medical consultation.

titre: Very bad setup for medical help
texte: One very glaring issue right away is you can’t select pre-existing conditions (it only asks if you’re a smoker or have diabetes and it only asked if I was pregnant AFTER starting the assessment [and this was even after setting it to no MYSELF already!]) I am diagnosed epileptic; I have been since I was 14, and pre-existing conditions and the medications for them can severely change the diagnosis of a person’s issues. Then again, this app can’t even take medication into account, either. I wouldn’t trust any output it gives since it wouldn’t be medically accurate unless the person happened to be perfectly healthy to begin with. And even then it still wouldn’t be guaranteed to be accurate at all.

titre: Programmed very well.. but could use some minor improvements
texte: I’m diagnosed with bipolar disorder and ada has been able to help me distinguish between when I’m having an anxiety attack or when my muscle aches and pains were more than just side effects of my meds. However, I believe that ada could be improved if we were able to add pre existing conditions to our profiles, in addition to our prescriptions actually factoring into the reports. Am I feeling fatigued because of my meds? Or is it something else? Pre existing conditions and medications can make it that much harder to question if it’s more than a side effect. And especially in the times of Covid seeking medical help is just getting more expensive in the US. But ada has helped me see when I should and shouldn’t go to the doctor (they helped me recognize the symptoms of pneumonia in time for me to get diagnosed before my lungs would be damaged any further than they were). I love this program, I just wish we could add the illnesses we already have and the effects our meds have on us. Of course the devs can’t list every medication, but letting us list the symptoms those meds cause should be factored into ada’s reports. There’s more conditions other than smoking, diabetes, etc that ada currently lists, and I feel the preexisting conditions are very limited. And I hope my thoughts on this are seen by the devs and can be brought into consideration.

I love ADA. Some may not understand, but I suffer from bad anxiety about my health. It’s a new thing over the past few years and ADA has really helped me to stop my panicking (sometimes panic attacks). If I feel unwell or concerned I pop my symptoms into ADA and it is mostly nothing serious at all. This app not only indicates when something may need immediate medical attention, but it saves wasting NHS time for very minor issues that can be easily managed at home. It has helped me to feel more knowledgable about understanding my body and I can get some relief and feel at ease when I’m anxious about something by just popping on ADA, therefore I won’t make a GP appointment. I didn’t always go to the doctor and would tend to ignore symptoms but with this app I don’t need to. 

Great app would highly recommend x

ofc don't rely on this app in place of a doctor, but it's a great app to help gauge what you should do given your symptoms. a lot of the time, it'll give you a possible explanation that seems completely left field, but this only ever really happens with less common conditions. if you're like me and you're prone to getting sick more often than others, and your doctors seem to be sick of you & don't know what to do with you (😂), this app is extremely helpful for outlining potential diagnoses and figuring out when to bother your GP. 

Ada also has a conditions library, citing reliable and high quality sources, including links to the NHS page for that specific condition - so you can read up on the things suggested to you in your symptom assessment report! 

common sense, but, never use this as a substitute for medical attention. Ada gives you SUGGESTIONS, not ANSWERS.

A few years back I had an awful pain on the right side of my stomach & had extreme nausea, Ada asked me loads of questions regarding my symptoms and it indicated that I could possibly have appendicitis! I managed to get a doctors appointment that day and got sent to the hospital for a scan to check my appendix, it turns out I did have appendicitis & had to have surgery the next morning before it got any worse! Please remember ada doesn’t diagnose you, it gives you rough ideas on what you could possibly have wrong with you. I would definitely recommend this app. It was a life saver, literally!

This is NOT google medicine. As a healthcare practitioner I know this app asks all the questions I would think to ask during a analysis of patients symptoms and it also does them in a gentle and delicate manner. Usually in the UK we can only speak to a document over the phone for a triage appointment and this has been the case since long before the COVID-19 lockdown stared. We have 10 minutes on the phone with a patient on a phone call to decide whether we need to see them for a physical examination and tests or issue a prescription or make a referral. It’s not really medicine anymore. This app does as good a job as most doctors and nurses can do over the phone in lieu of a physical examination. 
 A lot of people tend to panic when they research their symptoms. I think this app is more helpful and calming than reading countless websites which are so generic and non-specific. The data analysis is clear and further valid information is provided and presented in a professional and supportive manner. 
I’d say it’s worth the money and I look forward to the apps development. I think apps like this will replace a lot of initial patient contact to prioritise and collect data for treatment and track symptoms.

titre: Ada sends your health data to third parties
texte: Avoid!!! While the app itself is great, it shares your health data with Third parties like Amplitude who then are able to build user profiles, all without telling users or asking for consent! This company is completely untrustworthy!!!!

Went to my doctor over some kidney pain, was told the usual: exercise, sleep properly and stay hydrated. Nothing changed. Lower back and abdomen still in moderate pain, thumping headache that comes and goes.

Downloaded this app, told me I had a kidney infection. Now, usually I’m quite skeptical of AI, however, I went back to the doctor and showed them the medical report that the app recommends you show, and my god this AI was spot on. Doctor interviewed me a little more about what was happening and then apologised for previously downplaying my symptoms, I walk away with an antibiotics course that prevented a kidney infection from developing further.

I am now fully behind medical AI as I’ve first hand experienced how effective it is. You can say goodbye to doctors completely in about 20 years, they are honestly useless! With time this AI only gets better, as it has more data to train with. 

Fair warning to anyone deciding what to study at college - stay away from medical diagnosis.

Ada is such an amazing app! I’ve recently been having trouble with my tail bone. It’s hurting like crazy, especially when I try and get off of a chair after sitting down for a pro-longed amount of time. This issue has been going on for around 3 weeks now.

I got worried, so I asked Ada. It asks you a series of helpful questions. There are no ads, no subscriptions. It’s great. The assessments don’t take long. At the end of the assessment, Ada will recommend some health-care facilities near you that you could visit. Then, it shows you a bunch of possible causes, and whether you need to go to an emergency care, seek medical care or whether it can be usually treated at home. After a while, Ada will ask you if your symptoms are better or the same. 

Even if you don’t think your suffering from any type of medical condition, Ada is a great app to have nonetheless. You don’t have to give them a single penny, and not one single ad pops up. It downloads super fast.

Once you click on each possible medical condition, it has an arrow showing you why it might be that. For example, Ada asked me do I have redness on my tail bone, I selected yes, therefore there is an arrow pointing to why the app thinks it could be that. It’s great for those people who hate visiting the doctors whenever they got a symptom, so they can have peace of mind. (I’m not saying don’t visit the doctor, but I’ve had experiences where the app knows better than the health-care professionals).

Please try it and see it for yourself. Even better than some doctors. I cannot put to words how accurate it is. 
Thank you very much for developing this application.

I used this App when I had a nasty throat infection and couldn’t get an appointment to see my doctor, the throat infection consisted of ulcerated blisters at the back of my throat and on my tonsils etc, I used Ada to track my symptoms and it came back with “Herpangina” (not an STD even though the name suggests it lol) in the end my wife took me to A&E at our local hospital as 1, I couldn’t get an appointment to see my doctor in time, and 2, it was incredibly painful to eat/swallow/drink which resulted in me losing weight and if I’d of gone any longer dehydration. Whilst in A&E the nurse just put it down to “throat infection” (in other words she didn’t know). I suggested could it possibly be Herpangina, to which the nurse in the A&E department had never heard of, and while she I was there she had researched it on her computer and comparing images on the internet along with symptoms with me she said, “you’re right, it is!” And that in “40 years of her being a nurse she had never heard of it” in the end, Herpangina was my diagnostic, so thanks to Ada I was able to get the right treatment for it.
But all in all Ada is an amazing app, I would 100% recommend!

Now it goes all over the place, comes to wild conclusions and even gathered false info in the symptoms listed in the report. Truly upsetting to see the downgrade.

It’s not really AI. It’s more like 20 questions. You just answer yes or no and by process of elimination it will spit out 5 or 6 possible explanations for your symptoms. You can’t talk to it like you can talk to AI. You can’t give more detail or background. It doesn’t ask about medication or history. It’s really old fashioned compared to all the AI apps out there.

They kept me in overnight and put me on hard core blood thinners for life now ( since I’ve had 3 DVT’s in the last 3 decades but it’s moved up from the legs to the lungs now. After checking the app for the worst possible scenarios, my symptoms usually subside fairly quickly. All in all, a great app.

The app’s simplistic approach allows for a clean, focused and helpful information tool. 

Can also be used to assess symptoms of others without having to get the patient to download, create an account, etc. and without merging their medical data with yours. 

One of the most transparent privacy policies I’ve ever seen! In general the app prompts you to opt-in, rather than opt-out to certain data analytics. In addition, there’s always a direct link to the specific section that governs the particular feature you’re about to opt-in (next to the toggle) in a human readable format. In other words, you won’t find a generically vague “we use some or all of the data we collect for parts or all of our services that we offer now or later in the future.” privacy policy, and you’re actually in control of your data.

I am a pretty anxious person, which can be a blessing and a curse at times. this app helps me double check that i am doing okay and calm myself down about some symptoms i shouldn’t worry about (of course, if you are still unsure it never hurts to speak to a doctor). this app also helped save my grandma’s life once when i was sure her symptoms were indicators of an upcoming stroke but i was not sure. she almost had me leave for the day so she could get some rest but i was sure she was not alright and i checked and it advised to see emergency medical attention. we were lucky enough to be on time to save her! i would strongly recommend this app to anyone.

titre: Works great for common medical conditions...
texte: Wow! I am a physician in Canada and have been testing this app for the de by patient me at our walk-in/urgent care clinic. I’ve tested it with symptoms from many common conditions and the app does a GREAT job of identifying the common, not-so-serious conditions we see regularly. I’m still not convinced that it replaces “everything” I do as a family physician (still very biomedically focused, doesn’t explore health issues from a holistic perspective, and doesn’t offer opportunistic preventive screening), BUT... I’ll admit I get a little worried about job security when I see an app like this out there for everyone to use.

titre: Perfect for those with health anxiety!
texte:
I have panic disorder and health anxiety and one of my unfortunate compulsions during a panic attack was googling symptoms, as we have all experienced, dr google told me i have various rare life threatening illnesses which only worsened the panic. 

I discovered Ada and haven’t looked back since!! it doesn’t tell me i have some rare disease and i love the fact it factors in mental health! it’s so reassuring to see “panic attack” pop up after describing how i’m feeling :)

This app looks like it’s good and it seems more thorough than other symptom checkers.  But I’ve only used it briefly and I’ve already found a few problems. Firstly a back arrow in the symptom checker would be great. It’s annoying to have to go back to the beginning because you tapped the wrong answer. Second, the symptom tracker wasn’t able to identify my problem. When I tried to write more information I couldn’t type in the box. The keyboard wouldn’t come up. This was very frustrating.

I’ve been using it for years now, it’s my go to when I’m not feeling well before heading straight to the gp. First time I used it, it correctly suggested the condition I was diagnosed with weeks later. It helps me figure out when symptom flares are very likely an existing condition, so I know which specialist to head to. Doesn’t replace a formal diagnosis of course, but it’s a great starting point and can help you figure out the important information to tell your doctor as it asks so many clarifying questions.
As a medical student, I find this tool especially useful to locate holes in my reasoning and history taking skills and to gain knowledge of other, fringe diagnoses for many presentations.

AI Helps me to understand my symptoms.
Been using this app for years and it never ceases to amaze me with its accuracy!

titre: Very logical and objective, great for hypochondriacs!
texte:
Being a self-admitted hypochondriac, this app is fantastic and doesn’t evoke feelings of panic and worry like when googling your symptoms. It appears to be a very logical and evidence-based approach to any combination of symptoms. 

The questions are short, simple and relevant. The report created at the end of the questionnaire is objective, transparent and is backed by statistical evidence. For example, it’ll list a number of possible conditions and say something like: 

*name of condition*
Seek emergency care
6 out of 10 people with your symptoms had this condition

*name of condition*
Seek medical advice
15 out of 1000 people with your condition had this condition 

It also gives you a history of your “assessments” which it clarifies ARE NOT medical diagnoses, in case you need to ever show your doctor or emergency department.

Very impressed!!! Will definitely continue to use this app if I’m ever concerned about my health! 🩺😁👍

I want to start by saying this app isn't like websites such as "webMD" .. it's not going to frighten you into believing you have had a brain aneurism, when you're really just a little dehydrated and have a headache. 

This app actually feels like I am talking with a doctor. It will build a basic profile about you (smoking status, age, etc.) when you first sign up, and then every time you use it from then it will ask you for your symptoms, and then it will go through and ask you relevant questions based on those. For the final result, it uses what I imagine to be a process of elimination, as well as statistics from other users (don't worry, complete patient confidentiality applies, you don't see anybody else's info ever!) and their health results.. Then it will give you a list of few most likely cases that it could be, statistics on how many others with the same symptoms ended up having this condition, as well as saves the whole assessment for you so that you can show it to your doctor if you decide to go, that way you won't ever forget to mention anything to him either. 

This app can not stress enough that their results are NOT intended to act as a diagnosis, and that contact with medical professionals are always essential if you're ever concerned about something.

titre: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 5 Stars — The Best Health Tracking App I’ve Used
texte:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Best diabetes and health tracking app I’ve used

MySugr has been an outstanding app for keeping all of my health data organized in one place. I use it daily to track my blood glucose, meals, carbs, activity, blood pressure, weight, and even A1C trends. The app is very easy to use, clean, and makes logging fast.

One of the best features is that it syncs seamlessly with Apple Health, which makes it even more powerful. I love being able to see my glucose readings alongside my steps, workouts, weight, and other health data from my Apple Watch and iPhone all in one ecosystem.

The reports and trends are excellent and help me clearly see how fasting, meals, exercise, and daily habits affect my numbers. It has made me much more consistent and accountable with my health goals.

If you use Apple devices and want a reliable way to track glucose and overall wellness, this app is absolutely worth it. Highly recommend!

What I really appreciate is being able to look back at trends and actually see how my food choices, fasting, exercise, and supplements affect my numbers over time. That kind of insight keeps me motivated and accountable. The reports and graphs are excellent, and syncing everything together makes managing my health so much easier.

If you’re serious about tracking your glucose and overall wellness, this app is worth every bit of 5 stars. Highly recommended!

I have used this application for years, routinely using it to record my bg every mealtime as part of the rituals we know and love. It has kept my data safe all this time and is not too annoying once you turn off the monster sounds. 

Today I settle down for my main meal and the app has decided that I cannot eat until I have re-logged in and agreed to the terms and conditions. My iPhone hasn’t stored the password so I’m supposed to faff with accounts while my food gets cold. So I don’t bother. 

With makes me think again, why am I doing this? What is in the terms and conditions?

So I read it and sure enough, they will ‘share’ my data with third parties, probably use it to train ai’s and whatnot. So why am I doing the logging, for my benefit, for other diabetics, or so Roche can squeeze more profits out?

I imagine all the apps do this, but now, just by annoying me once, I’m not so accepting of this particular app any more.

titre: What have you done? It’s useless to me now!

titre: Very good app in the past, now? Not so
texte:
***** Update ***** 09/03/24 
The new transition to the new owners has been very fiddly with the account changes. It constantly asks for you to log in and doesn’t seem to remember that you did. Also the estimation of hba1c which was very useful has disappeared. This has only been in the last release or so. It was what I was still using the app for so this means I will stop using it. Thus they will use the detailed data they were using for analysis from at least one user. This could be a mistake on their part or a move to get users to go Pro. There isn’t anything in pro that I would use as I’m using a pump. Is very useful I imagine if you are still using injections. Not very useful in its current form if not. The calculator is redundant for modern day pump users as the pumps do this automatically. The reports don’t really give any analysis and the advice section doesn’t give any other suggestions other than “you are going low at nights. This can be due to taking too much insulin.” Not very helpful. They send a report out by email each week telling you that you have done some thing against a monster but since it’s not capturing you against anything else then what is the purpose behind the email? Not sure what it is helping me to know. Overall, was a good app (see previous review below) but now just a way to waste time. 

The new clear screen telling you what bolus to take and the calculation is great. I have just one request of the developers which is why I can only give it four stars. I’m a pump user and sometimes I need to have an Extended bolus over a couple of hours. If I need to give one of those the calculations are difficult to do. Please can the devs work out multi wave doses ( a few units up front and then an extended bolus over an hour or two) and extended doses. This would be the most important update needed. Just to say that the support is good. They respond quite quickly given the world we now live in. They can resolve issues quickly. Thanks

At first I just enjoyed not having to do handwriting notes and became curious of all the things it was possible to enter. So when suddenly my levels were unstable for days I would increase my levels and sometimes I couldn’t remember how many units I had taken the day before.

I added an entry for my insulin type Slow Release and the quantity. Tressiba Pen are kept in the fridge and after the first use you may keep it at room temperature for up to 40 days and I did. The app allowed me to realize that after 28 days I had increased my intake of insulin from 12 to 14 and when I reached 16, then I would start a new Pen for 1.5 year, analyzing my entries allowed me to see the problem; so I started a new Pen and kept it in the fridge and it lasted 42 days and interestingly my morning readings stabilized between 4.5 and 5.1 no more 6 to 7.

It also keep track of the time it read the entry and I noticed that between 7:30 AM and 8 I had better readings and on lazy days 8:45 to 9:30 bad readings and now I have set a reminder for 7:30.

MySugr recently sent a mea culpa email to all users impacted by a change to the app that impacted its usability. With the apology sent, and the app functionality restored, I felt it necessary to undo my negative review as the app functions again. 

The premise of this app is simple; all your diabetes info and logging in one place. It pulls in data from certain CGMs (not FreeStyle Libre) and combines it with data from Apple Health. With the addition of your own Carb details (and meal details, if you’re so inclined) you can review a much much more holistic view of your blood glucose than you could otherwise. Want to know how that meal from your favourite restaurant impacted BG last time? Just search for the meal / restaurant and check how it played out. 

Why 4 stars? Integration with FreeStyle Libre (which is admittedly likely due to Abbott as much as anyone else) hold its back slightly as you have to enter BG readings manually. Typically you’re entering carb counts at the same time, so it’s workable. (Also, it can read BG measurement from Apple Health if your CGM logs to Apple) 

Further, there’s a few easy-win features missing: Siri Shortcuts would be very helpful, as would CarPlay (so I can use my car’s touchscreen to start a log entry). 

In reality it’s more like a 4.75 star app. Highly recommended.

titre: Très bonne application mais quelques améliorations nécessaires
texte:
En général, c’est une très bonne application. En fait, la meilleure de toute pour le suivi du diabète. J’adore!  Cependant, il y a quelques améliorations nécessaires les voici:
1. Utilisation de Google comme carte ou moteur de recherche pour trouver les points d’interêt plus rapidement.
2. Utilisation d’une base d’aliment avec lecteur de codes barres afin de pouvoir importer les valeurs de glucides automatiquement.
3. Augmentation de la personnalisation des rapports comme la possibilité de faire afficher la tension artérielle, et quand nous avons pris les médicaments.
4. Possibilité d’ajouter le pouls aux donnés de tension artérielle.
5. Possibilité d’ajouter la saturation en oxygène. 
Superbe application

titre: Spend more time on useful reports and less on cutesy giggle emojis
texte:
The target range is too generic and only allows an overall range.  Not powerful enough.  Needs to provide before and after meal ranges with critical highs and lows.  Competition offers this. 

Icon tagging is nice and offers extensive variety of options.   Would be cool to offer user defined icons with import icon graphic option. 

Paired with the Accu-Chek Guide meter which is the intended meter for this software.  However the AccuChek Meter which had shown me a pattern analysis of high glucose mornings over a few days , the app does not import that pattern information, nor does the MySugr software analytics calculate its own or similar pattern which the Accu-Chek meter finds.  

Reports could use improving, does not include Blood Pressure which is imported via Apple Health.   

Reports only provide overall weekly, monthly glucose averages etc. Basic information is pretty standard these days , but eliminates delving into deeper analysis.  No weekly before , fasting , or after meal glucose averages 

Competitor apps provide this feature.
Makes my life a bit easier either great reporting feature.

titre: Every Health data on one app
texte: Good Application For overall Health.

Compared to Contour App and tester:-
*AccuChek has the best test strips container.
*Contour test strips better to apply blood to with much fewer failures.
*MySugr app test result transfer slightly erratic whereas Contour is 100% reliable.
*MySugr App allows wildly erroneous test results - e.g. from contaminated finger - to be deleted, Contour does not.
*Data entry for tests is inefficient and structured for the points scoring system - which l don’t care about. Just to put in the minimum basic info of fasting/after meal/ before meal l have to scroll down past all the other stuff and to expand the menu selection to find the one I want. Contour App is much, much better - the whole menu just presented as soon as the reading is taken, and the Contour four time groups anticipate the meal being tested for, very intuitive.
*Contour status display is excellent, allows easy scrolling by day groups to see day-over-day results. MySugr version requires scrolling down one day at a time and is not graphic and less intuitive.
*MySugr daily deviation range data reading is excellent.
*Contour App uses colour and graphics much better than MySugr which is virtually monotone and dull.
*After having evaluated the Contour App for 100 tests and the MySugr App for about 30 so far I rate Contour 4.5 and MySugr 2.5

titre: Amazing app – just one small issue with the graph
texte:
MySugr has been a fantastic tool for managing my Mum’s diabetes. It’s easy to use, reliable, and has made tracking her blood sugar levels so much simpler over time. We really love it and use it daily.

The only issue is the graph – it’s not very customisable, and the default version is too short/narrow. Other than that, this app is absolutely brilliant. Highly recommend!

It communicates without any issues between Apple Health and mySugr, on occasion the nurses would use the hospital BGM and after entering it would update immediately in mySugr. 
The last three days I used mySugr to assist the treating physician in rehab to establish an interaction between my BSL and the new medication I was taking. 
All in all a highly recommended APP and it’s for free people!

texte:

It feels like it does a lot but not enough to be practically useful certainly not for Australian markets. I don’t even understand the points stuff when i log! 

Too many pharma companies competing for attention not enough coordination.  for example I use: Libre to measure and track bsl, novodisk novo pen 6 to log insulin intake, my sugr to calculate bolus, my fitness pal to figure out nutrition information. It’s exhausting

texte: Parfait pour moi

J’aime beaucoup cette application qui est bien pensée même si elle peut être améliorée sur certains points : il devrait être possible d’avoir un tableau affichant les glycémies en fonction des moments de la journée. 
Pourquoi une mauvaise note donc alors je juge cette application très bien ? Tout simplement car Roche propriétaire de cette app, ne propose toujours pas en France la prise en charge des lecteurs Bluetooth de la marque. Roche nous renvoi vers Gluci-Chek qui est très inférieure à MySugr (pas de compatibilité avec Santé entre autres).

Update 2025 :
L’application est compatible depuis plusieurs mois avec les lecteurs Guide. 
Mais il manque encore des fonctionnalités : courbes en mode paysage, glycémies en fonction des moments de la journée, pourquoi pas une base de données alimentaire afin de choisir les aliments des repas et en extraire les quantités de glucides.

Pour le moment cette application reste ma référence en la matière.

I find the ‘creature’ and challenges a bit gimmicky, but would like to have a free text diary area to add notes about diet etc. I don’t count carbs but would like to be able go back and see what has worked. Auto off doesn’t work on my device (which is probably caning the battery a bit) and the comment area maybe could have added flexibility.  Apart from that I am really pleased to have an app that supports the hardware so well.

Je vais investir dans un accu-check mobile (auto piqueur et lecteur combiné avec 50 languettes pré-charger) qui va me permettre de synchroniser mes résultats capillaire automatiquement sur l’application.

Je tiens à remercier vivement accu-check pour cette application et j’espère qu’il vont continuer de développer leurs matériels et leur application.

Permet de noter plein d'infos utiles pour le suivi et l'analyse de ma fille diabétique de type 1. Permet également de synchroniser avec le smartphone des parents. Les défauts: 1- pour analyser sa journée il faut faire défiler de gauche à droite pour les infos les plus importantes, elles ne sont pas affiches part défaut ( comme par exemple les doses d'insuline injectées). 2- le format d'export pdf des données est très joli mais beaucoup moins efficace et lisible que les formats en tableau classiques papier, il n'était pourtant pas difficile d'imiter les aspects les plus utiles (concision, comparison jour à jour) des glucographes papier. 3-on n'a pas accès à nos données en ligne !!!!!!! C'est rageant !! On peut voir les données sur un téléphone, mais le site Web prétend que l'application est indisponible d'avis notre pays !!! Donc on peut payer l'abonnement, mais on ne peut pas voir nos données sur le Web, on peut uniquement les exporter en un format pdf coloré mais très mal adapté à une étude comparative des jours et semaines. Je suis très déçu. J'aime bien le design, le principe et l'entreprise derrière, mais les défauts sont tellement gros que je suis à la recherche d'une alternative qui me permette de lire mes données sur le Web...

Simple d'utilisation elle est très complète et surtout ludique, ce qui n'est pas souvent la cas dans le suivi d'un traitement quotidien. Et l'équipe support  est également à l'écoute et très réactive. Bravo et merci au nom de tous les diabétiques ! Isabelle

Après avoir longtemps cherché et essayé les diverses applications de carnet de suivi, je pense avoir enfin trouvé l'application parfaite. En effet, celle-ci est simple d'utilisation, très esthétique, et complète. Je remercie l'équipe ayant développé cette application, ils m'ont permis de faciliter le suivi de mon diabète. Merci !
Une application bien pratique qui permet de suivre ses glycémies de manière ludique et motivante.

C'est l'application qu'il me fallait pour me motiver à me surveiller régulièrement. Elle est vraiment complète.

J'adore l'intégration automatique des donnée CGM et sport de façon automatique (on règle l'app. 1 fois et après c'est le carnet qui se remplit tout seul :-) J'adore ! Et bien sûr le calculateur de Bolus, trop bien. Mais désormais j'adore et je kiff les rapports. Merci.
Une super appli complète et amusante.
Grâce à l appli je gère vraiment mieux mon diabète. Merci
J'aime beaucoup l'application elle m'aide vraiment tout les jours a visualiser ou j'en suis avec ma glycémie. Merci a l'équipe de m'y sugr.

titre: La qualité a malheureusement un prix
texte:
Je possède l'application depuis deux semaines maintenant et j'ai, grâce à son interface ludique, fonctionnelle et design, j'ai fait plus de glycémies en 15 jours qu'en 15 mois...
Allez savoir pourquoi un simple jeu avec une personnification de la maladie sous forme d'un petit monstre presque mignon me donne l'envie de faire ce que j'ai dénigré pendant presque 5 ans (mes glycémies)

Les points essentiels recherchés dans un carnet électronique sont présents, l'interactivité est bel et bien là (graphiques, couleurs, photos, informations psycho). 

J'en aurai pour plus de paragraphes encore pour y expliquer les bienfaits de Carnet tant ils sont évidents.

En revanche, je m'attarderai sur  le prix de l'application qui reste, malgré moi onéreux.

2,99€ / mois ou environ 24€ / an me semblent considérables pour une solution presque miracle au suivi du diabète et à l'intermédiaire que peut créer Carnet entre le patient et son diabètologue. On se sent presque forcés de passer à la version pro, comme des non-voyants à qui l'on ferait payer l'éducateur pour leurs chiens d'aveugle

Merci tout de même.

Cette version est complètement buggée, plus de transmission avec Health, les 3 derniers jours se nomment "hier", au prix de l'abonnement la réactivité devrait être meilleur pour régler de tels bugs, car ça fait du coup quasi 1 semaine qu'il n'y a plus de transmission entre Health et l'app

Simple et complet 
Statistiques et challenges

titre: Un vent de fraîcheur dans le domaine du suivi du diabète
texte:
Diabétiques de tout type, ne cherchez plus : vous avez ici le meilleur outil de suivi jamais conçu pour smartphone. Un carnet de suivi complet et original comprenant notamment une interface très claire, moderne et franchement jolie, des fonctionnalités de suivi nombreuses et pourtant simple d'utilisation, ou encore un système de reporting au top. Sans oublier ce mini monstre et ses adorables petits gémissements qui amusent beaucoup mon entourage !
Seul regret qui justifie l'absence de la dernière étoile : pour accéder à toutes les autres fonctionnalités et retirer la pub il faut disposer d'un compte pro à financer par abonnement... Pourquoi ne pas l'inclure en achat in-app définitif ? Dommage, on n'était pas loin du sans faute ! ;/
Bonne continuation à toute l'équipe !

Motivante drôle et trop Mimi!
Très pratique et détaillée même pour diabète de type1 sous pompe!
Différencie les bolus de repas et les bolus de correction!

Très complète, je n'ai pas encore tout exploité. Il manque par contre des icônes pour décrire l'alimentation( il faudrait que cela soit plus précis pour les différents féculents et les différents apports glucidiques)
Mon appli favorite. Juste remettre la prise de médicaments en direct sans passer par le menu mais c'est un détail. Juste parfait

Cet app est très pratique et en évolution constante. 
L'équipe MySugr prend en compte les remarques. 
Même le blog est une source d'informations intéressantes. 

Je conseille vivement cette app à la pointe.

Depuis 3 semaines cette appli m'est d'une grande aide pour gérer mon taux de glycémie en visualisant les résultats par jour, semaine, quinzaine et mois.
Très bon et beau travail à toute l'équipe. Continuez, merci.

titre: Bonne appli, tres pratique, a ameliorer
texte:

Beaucoup plus complet aussi.

Il y a aussi un souci pour imprimer les photos.
Cela sera corrigé prochainement, espérons le, car l'appli sur iPhone, c'est vraiment pratique.

Depuis l'amélioration de l'appli c'est génial.

Les entrées sont très rapides à renseigner et on peut y ajouter beaucoup d'éléments descriptifs (exercice, moment de la journée, aliments etc...). Le rendu est clair et c'est une aide non négligeable à la gestion du diabète. Seul suggestion: ajout de plus d'entrées et raffiner les catégories pour les aliments. Bref je suis très satisfaite.

titre: App is fantastic but, missing carb counter
texte: The mysugr app is great, and with the camera capturing for bg results, it is even easier to keep track of everything. However, a carb counting food database would complete the deal. It takes to long to go through bg testing, one app for counting carbs for meal, administering insulin, another app for registering it all. You have been updating the app incredibly, please add this feature.

My endocrinologist recommended this app over the one I was previously using. The reports are useful for my appointment with her. 

The price is quite high compared to other apps but the response from the support team is quick. The app has some nice features (and a few features to add still :-). The best feature so far for me is the timed reminder to take a BG measurement. That really helps get consistent timing after meals.

[update] the app has been updated a lot since I last reviewed it. The biggest new feature for me is the recording of tablets. This could be slightly improved by making it possible to record multiple pills in one go rather than one pill at a time.

[update for v3.0] wow! MySugr have really gone to town on the app this time. I love it. They've fixed the issue with multiple pills (see above). The new look is great. New tags to match the web app. And the search feature is also pretty cool. Great job. :-)

Ainsi à jeun ( avant le repas) la glycémie devrait de situer entre 80 et 120 et deux heures après le repas, pour la post prandiale entre 110 et 140 donc pas moyen de mettre des mini et maxi correspondant sans les inclure avec les à jeun...

L'intégration aux iDevice et la prise en compte des opportunités offertes par les nouvelles technologie sont quasi parfaite. Je n'attend plus qu'un calculateur de Bolus en fontion des glycémies et des glucides, ainsi que l'importation des données iBGStar et ce sera parfait ! Ah si ! Ça serait bien de pouvoir paramétrer des alertes récurrentes (injections quotidiennes de l'insuline basale) et automatiques dans un délai déterminé après une hypo ou une hyper.

Un vrai carnet  sur lequel il est possible de noter les bases pour porteurs de pompes, un rapport hyper complet que l'on peut envoyer par mail,on peut y décrire ses repas et y noter les glucides et bolus, les corrections éventuelles, ajouter à tout  moment un goûter ou une hypo, bref, c'est vraiment pensé pour nous :)

texte: I pay $25 a year for no ads. But yet occasionally will get an ad.  Kinda defeats the purpose of paying.

My experience with this app in the month I've used it has been overly positive. I was directed to it by a GoodRX article. But, there are two things I would change.

Please make the baseline mood "Okay" instead of "Very bad." 

Please set the medication recording time to the current time, instead of the reminder time. I have a reminder set for 6am, but if I try to record the medication at 9:37am, I would like that to be the time that it suggests. Ir at least make changing this baseline an option. I typically record my medication immediately after taking it, but I do not always take it when my reminder goes off. I'm tired of scrolling every time. This was something the Apple Health app did that I really appreciated.

I stopped using Apple health because I plan to switch to an android device soon. I prefer apps that work for both interfaces.

The user interface is clean and easy to understand, I love the wide variety of symptoms that i can track, and the ability to track appointments. I also like that this app for unnecessary personal information when I downloaded the app. It didn't hit me with a paywall or an ad for its upgraded version either. And I never see ads on this free version! (Maybe that's because of an adblocker I have? But I don't think so)

The fact that they let me know when my phone is on silent mode is a neat bonus as well.

The app is sleek in appearance. It works smoothly, I like its animations and color palette. I've encountered no bugs in the month I've begun to use it, and it's done exactly what I needed it to do. I use MyTherapy, Routinery, and Finch to help me complete my mourning routines and maintain my mental health. The reminders are never annoying, wonderful at standing out amongst my other notifs, and i didn't even have to set them up like that myself.

It is a new med that’s only been out for a bit, so I can’t really be upset. I just want someone to see this to maybe update the app to include the new Wegovy tablets

I kept forgetting my meds because of the demands on my time.  Since downloading this app, I haven’t forgotten!  The app has a pleasant tone that reminds me even when my phone isn’t near by.  The notifications on the home screen mean that even if I don’t hear the tone I get a visual reminder that hovers in the background letting me know it is time for meds.  This app is just what I needed to ensure I don’t miss meds I 💯 percent recommend.

titre: Excellent App! Love the Refill Reminders and the progress charts.
texte:
I really rely on the MyTherapy App. I have chronic pancreatitis (CP) with experimental pancreatic insufficiency (EPI) and bile acid malabsorption (BAM). This means I take a lot of medications. Some are scheduled and some are “as needed” depending on my symptoms. 

One of my favorite features is the refill reminders. I log when I get my new prescription and how many pills are in the bottle and have it give me a reminder when I get down to a certain number of pills. 
Now I never run out of medications! Particularly ones that have to be ordered by the pharmacy and not always immediately available. 

Another favorite is the progress charts. They let me see at a glance if I have taken my schedules medications and how much as needed pain and nausea medication I have taken. 

The reports let me give my physician a PDF as needed. And makes it much easier for us to discuss treatment regiments especially in conjunction with the symptoms tracker. 

Support team is also available and responsive within 24 hours. 
Thank you to the App makers for producing and maintaining this critical app. It has been a gamechanger as my disease progresses and helps relieve anxiety over medication and keeps me on track.
Just love the app it keeps track of my activity with the pills when I’ve taken them and it works very well. I take different medication several times a day so it really helps. Keep track.
This app is good for notifications, but it doesn’t allow for notes such as site location for injections or details of the medication being taken.

Jacek worked diligently with me in troubleshooting the app. He stayed in direct contact with me until the app operated correctly. We discovered the problem was that I was closing the app out completely when the app needs to stay open and running quietly n the background. Don’t worry this will not affect your battery usage at all.  I am very happy to have this app since I need to be reminded several times a day to take my medications on time. The app also has other functions that you can tailor to your own needs. I have complete confidence in the Meds & Pill Reminder App. I highly recommend it.

this app is so good and allows you to create your own treatment plan and sends everyday reminders.

titre: From Overwhelmed to Organized: The App Every Caregiver Needs
texte:
This is my first year caring for my mom, and MyTherapy has been a game-changer. I started out just wanting a pill minder, but this app does so much more — from tracking medication inventory and refill reminders to graphing health data like blood sugar, blood pressure, mood, pain, and more. All I have to do is consistently log the info, and the app turns it into clear trends I can share with her doctors.

Every doctor we’ve met has been impressed with the records I’ve provided. After 10 months, I can’t believe it’s still free — it has truly made caregiving so much easier.

Highly recommend to any caregiver or patient who wants peace of mind!
The best app I found to track not only medication’s, but also symptoms of a large variety
Love this app. Easy to use. Tracks inventory. Allows me to track when I take meds. Allows me to track my symptoms. Use it religiously!

BUT: the one and only suggestion I have is specifically for interval medications. If I’m late on my medication, the interval should reflect that! For example, my medication is taken once weekly. If I take my dose on Wednesday, then accidentally don’t take my next dose until Tuesday (13 days later rather than 7), I should not be reminded the very next day to take it again. It should remind me 7 days later.

Anyway, aside from that issue, this is a great app and I highly recommend it to anyone who takes medications and/or vitamins, even if you don’t have trouble remembering to take them!

titre: Adhd and busy life: very helpful!
texte: I always have had difficulty remembering to take medicine consistently, or remembering if I took it. Now, my doc and I are on a journey trying to figure out which new medicine is going to be a good fit for a new issue. Each different medicine we try has different requirements- morning or night, certain time span before or after other meds, etc. I’m already taking other meds with requirements. This plus a busy lifestyle with many other responsibilities = a brain that can’t always keep up. I’ve tried taking notes on a note app, but it’s clunky to look through a list and try to keep track of everything. This app is perfect for my needs. Simple interface = quick and easy to use. I love that I get a reminder to take meds at the right time, but I get to log it at the time I actually took it. This makes it so much easier to space out the rest of my meds appropriately according to what I actually did. It also helps me remember what I did in the days before, so if I’m consistently off for a while I can slowly make it back to my normal schedule. Thank you to the developers for a great app! Would love if you make one just like it for me to use to keep track of my kids’ meds! When they all get sick, it’s a full time job to keep track of who can take what medicine, what dose, and what time. I love the clean, uncluttered interface of this medication log.
Since using them as a medication reminder, I’ve been much better about taking my vitamins and remembering how many I’ve taken.

I highly recommend getting this app and using its program.

titre: My Therapy Helps Me Stay on Track
texte:
I take 10 prescription meds and several otc meds and supplements. Keeping up with all this is impossible without some sort of tracking system. I have been using My Therapy to track my meds, exercise plan, blood pressure, refills etc for several years. I love it because it allows me to:

- keep a complete list all my meds, including meds used only one-time 
- set when they are due (day/time/frequency), dosage etc
- track exercise, blood pressure, symptoms 
- set alerts to remind me to take my meds,  check blood pressure, exercise 
- input that I’ve taken them or not 
- add and remove meds
- be able to quickly share with health care professionals all this information when I’m at an appointment 
- set alert to tell me when I need refills
- see a report over a period of months of anything being tracked;
And much more…
