I take so many tablets now that I need something to remind me, and also keep track of what my ‘inventory’ levels are (my drawer looks like a pharmacy). This free pill reminder works brilliantly for me, with multiple entries per day and customisable times and alerts. The only thing I can’t work out is how to change the times on an as hoc basis if, for example, I wake up late and need to shuffle everything back by an hour. Otherwise, brilliant, thank you for making this app!
This App certainly is very helpful in reminding me of taking my medication. Especially at the weekends when I get out of my routine. Very easy to set up and use.

This is a brilliant tool to make sure your medication is taken as prescribed, and also to keep together a wide variety of you medical details ready for when needed.
It is very straightforward and covers every eventuality that I have come across in making sure the right medication is taken at the right time.
An excellent piece of software, thank you.

This app does the job in reminding you to take your medication, and provides a good record of your history. The pill count is a good feature to manage your repeat prescriptions. The developers are responsive to making changing and fixing bugs.

titre: Excellent reminder app - and it’s free!
texte:
I’m terrible at remembering to take my medications, as I have ADHD, and this app helps remind me to take them perfectly. It has the notifs stay on your Lock Screen, so unlike other apps where I can just snooze the notifications if I don’t feel like doing it right then, it keeps reminding me every time I pick up my phone!
Plus there are no ads, despite it being free. Normally even the most innocuous free app absolutely hammers you with ads, but this is brilliant.
Plus it helps me keep on top of how many pills/doses I have of all my medications, and you can set it to warn you when you end up with only a week or so left of each med. This reminds me to re-order them from my doctor.
This app is excellent if you have a few different medicines to take , it’s audible feature is a subtle reminder of them
I love my digital reminder less chances of missing a dose
One of the most useful app on my phone. Helps me to take my medication on time.
This is exactly what I needed. Loved and happily donated
As someone with ADHD i love this app as it always reminds me to take my medication and tells me when I’m getting low and need more pills. Super helpful!
Really helping me!!

I started using this app as the old app I used would often tell
me I had a lot more meds left than I did. I even went back and manually calculated how many I’d logged since I last added a box and the number left I was being shown and it didn’t add, the same happened with this app multiple times now. I’ve added up all I’ve logged since adding my last pack of meds and that’s not the number that’s been deducted from my total meds (if that makes sense) 
I am in high levels of pain daily and suffer from brain fog etc and need help remembering when to order but as this doesn’t accurately reflect how many I have left I am often left with not enough pain meds, antidepressants etc. I’m going to have to give up on apps and use a spreadsheet

I started using the app after having an heart attack a few years ago and having to take lots of pills. It was easy to set up and great for remembering to take different pills at different times of the day. I recommend anyone who is taking pills to use the app.
The medication reminder is not always accurate in respect of the amount of medication remaining, so every so often I have had to do a manual check.

I absolutely love this it’s amazing at reminding me to take my meds and it’s totally free, it tracks what day you take them and if you put in the amount of pills you have left it gives you a reminder when you need to get more. I only tried one other app before trying this one and I didn’t even think twice about having another browse of free pill reminder apps. I also use it to remember to do everyday tasks as well. One of my favourite features though is the day streak, it motivates me to remember so much and I’ve only missed one day in my month of using this app and that was before I got into the routine of it. Love, love, love this app, it’s a must have.
Free pill reminder never forget to take ur pills again
I have been using for a few months now. It is really handy.  I get alerts to take my medicine so that I don’t forget.
Very good product to remind when to take my medicine.
Makes sure I don’t miss taking my meds
I’d be lost without this app to update and remind me when meds need renewing.

Does exactly what it says on the tin. Reminds you to take your medication at a time set up by you. Easy peasy and essential for those who find remembering to take meds an issue. It will also provide notifications if you haven’t taken it

Continues to issue reminders from the time the meds are due to be taken until you’ve confirmed you have taken them. 
It just doesn’t record the time the med was actually confirmed as taken, which would be helpful. But still good.

Does what it says on the tin. With a cute picture as a reward!
As with all these apps - it relies on you taking your meds when it reminds you!

Absolutely ideal. Reminder and stock control all-in-one PLUS so much more. You can use the features you want or use more - monitor your mood, monitor your symptoms plus it can help you with reports on taking medication (how efficient you are, how many times you’ve been late or missed a medication). Such a useful and beneficial app. Thanks!

titre: Best free pill reminder I’ve tried
texte: Does what it says on the tin. Lets me add medications that I take daily, weekly or on 12 days out of 30. No other app does it properly. This is the best free pill reminder I have found so far

The devs have kept this free app simple and clean. You can have regular reminders as well as record ad-hoc pull taking. The later feature useful after, say, surgery where you initially want painkiller reminders, but a few days later only need to record pill taking when needed. I decided to make a voluntary contribution as it made my recovery  easier.  Recommended

titre: Good app, doesn’t track quantities well
texte: I have been using the app for a few months to track quantities of several medications which are either taken daily/twice daily or as needed, so occasional use.  I noticed the quantity of a few didn’t always match the actual numbers and it’s only me who is here.  It kept happening, I kept a note and still it happens, a difference of 1 to 6 in a short time of a week or less.  I reached out in February, they asked so many questions which I couldn’t answer at the time, whether a manual entry medication or from the database and other actions which I couldn’t confirm, but it’s still happening, so I am trying a different app soon.
Discrete audio and visual reminder of when to take medication and to renew prescription

titre: Simple is best, it does exactly what it says and more
texte: I've tried multiple trackers and this is the best one I've found to stick with. Simple is best and you can track how many you have left, so you are aware of when you’ll run out. Also, it can give you a monthly report. I don't know where I’ll be without this app. I would have definitely missed days.
Very reliable and simple to use. Very happy.
Simple and effective app to keep track of your medicines.
Really nice, easy to use
This app is really good for reminding us if our requirement to have medication at set times of the day .
I have to take anti-rejection eye drops everyday and so grateful of this app.  It’s a tick box every day thank you

titre: Great free app - keeps you on track
texte: It was only in my 60s that I needed regular medication, so taking tablets morning and evening was a new habit. This app provides timely reminders. I love the free 'reward pic' at the end of the day when all doses are complete. The pill reminder, tracking inventory and reminding when prescriptions are needed, is a great help.

I find this app helpful for reminding me to take my medication and for keeping track of healthcare appointments. However, I have noticed a couple of issues that need to be fixed. Firstly, if your phone is on silent, you won't receive a reminder. Secondly, when adding the number of pills, the app only allows you to add a pack, and it doesn't let you confirm the exact amount.
free pill reminder - does exactly what i need

I have just got out of hospital with severe malnutrition and as such am taking a great number of new medications and supplements, including a liquid diet drink seven times a day. This app is phenomenal for being able to keep track of all of these. I use it in silent mode and the single little buzz is all I need to remind me that it is time for something to be taken. I also really appreciate that you can keep track of a variety of symptoms, though it would be helpful if you could add your own. This might be a bit much for some people, but I would also appreciate being able to keep track of bowel movements. 

However, I do have one critique, which is that I find it odd that activity that you log doesn’t appear in the charts section of the progress tab. Having to scroll all the way down my list of medications taken and symptoms logged in the list section to see my logged activity, and then having to remember those numbers to compare to other days when I scroll further is very unintuitive and unnecessarily difficult. Surely an easy way for logged activity to be shown in the charts section would be to make the upper limit the highest number logged for that specific activity? Please consider adding this feature if possible.
Free pill reminder
The free pill reminder is very easy to use and invaluable in helping me to take my medication at the right time.
Makes the world of difference with medication adherence, really helps
Great way of reminding you to take your medication.
My Brain for when I need to take multiple medications
Let’s you know when and what you need to take
Easy and intuitive and a great reminder app.

This app is a must if you are taking regular medication and you need a little reminder .
Let’s you know when and what to take and also how low you are so as to reorder your medication as and when required.
Perfect to remind me to take my medication if I’m busy. Easy set up. I’ve been using it for a good few years now and recommend it to friends
Having listed my prescriptions, I’m reminded by sound when to take them
Really helps me it reminds me to take my tablets.
This app has been so helpful for reminding me to take my various meds on specific days and dates, it’s been a life saver.

titre: Very helpful and practical app and medication reminder
texte: In the UK medications are often dispensed in multiples of 28 and to have 56 and 84 also as options would, in “Add package” , be helpful. This minor issue does not detract from the rating overall.
Very helpful if you take a number of medications

Really useful for pill reminder to order more but great for pills that are taken every other day or more randomly and the sound of the reminder sounds even when the phone is on silent.

I especially like the rewarding picture.

I downloaded this because a different one I used started to charge. This is a fantastic free pill reminder. It’s got different settings to suit different meds, and you can also add symptoms which is great to show your doctor. You can export the information that you’ve entered too which again, great for showing your doctor if needed. I love this app and recommend it to anyone. The best part is- you don’t need to pay for it but you have the option to donate to the creators, which I think is a great idea!

Brilliant app, really easy to use and it helps a lot with keeping track of medications! Its completely free to use and has a donation option which i used because I find it so useful! 10/10 cannot recommend enough!

Free Pill Reminder

A fantastic way to monitor and remember you medication! Reminders and quantity tracker!

I have tried a few medication-reminding apps, this is the best one I can find so far. It’s easy to use and have all important functions! One more thing that can improve is when a medication only use specific days of the week, but needs to be given twice a day, this cannot be set up now. I can only choose either “specific days of the week”, or “X times a day”, but cannot combine them. Other than that, this app is amazing!

titre: Pretty good day to day meds management
texte: I’ve used this and the built in health app mess management and this beats the built in features hands down. Easier to setup your meds and schedule, easier to log meds and to change the details if required.

titre: Good app, but far too embarrassing to have downloaded due to notifications
texte: As someone who would like to keep medication consumption a private matter, it’s not at all enjoyable to have medication reminders pop up whilst showing colleagues something on my phone, so I had to delete it.
Never forget your meds, keeps track of how many tablets left well in advance.
Randomly doesn’t take a tablet off so i’ve ran out of medication so you have to keep checking
Thanks to the reminder I no longer forget to take my meds and also don’t forget to put in repeat prescriptions due to the built in logging of quantities of meds left
Great for reminding of regular pills but also keeping track of anything additional you have to take.
I have learned how to use this properly now and it’s great

I've been using this for some time and I'm not sure how I could manage without it. I have up to 18 pills in the morning and 8 at night and some on different days of the week. This app reminds me what to take and when and let's me know inventory levels so I can get warnings to reorder before running out. Excellent!

This pill reminder ap has become a daily routine for me. It is reassuring to check off by meditation and track my mood each evening. I find the photographic reward particularly uplifting. Thank you for sourcing such a variety of colourful images!

It works but when get notifications on my watch says dismiss or confirm but neither of these work. So then mucks up the app as when confirmed on phone app when I get round to it or see it still has 1 on app does it for the next day instead. Try to sort it out but won’t do anything as close app and go back on and it’s back the way before. Needed this app to help remember when to take my copd medicine.
Great app well thought out pill reminder/ tablet reminder highly recommend to anyone managing their medications
Uso questa app ormai da più di 4 anni. È utile per non dimenticarsi l’assunzione giornaliera delle medicine e poi ti avvisa quando le stai finendo. Evitando così di restare senza medicine
Really fabulous tool
Free app that works well

titre: A great little free pill reminder app
texte: Easy to set up. Cute daily photos are a bonus.
Helping to keep on top of medical care
Have tried others but like this best.

I really like this and wish it was available when my parents were alive. I use it for medication appointments and recording measurements. There could be some improvements (nothing is perfect) to turn this into an even better app
Best app I’ve used to keep on track. I’d advise you to download & explore it! It’s very useful!
Have remembered my tablets since using the app.
A easy to use and free app with great features. Happy I migrated after my last app went subscription based 🙄

titre: Best free meds reminder and tracker
texte:
My previous pill tracker suddenly went to subscription. It’s not so complicated that I could justify paying so searched for another solution. This free pill and treatment reminder works even better than my previous one. I’m still working some things out but so far finding it great.
EDIT after a few months experience: I still love the simplicity of this free app. The only problem I have is that will a reducing dose of one of my meds it isn’t easy to programme variable doses/meds in advance. So I have to edit each day after checking my separate schedule. For that reason I have reduced my rating to a four star. Otherwise I have no complaints.
Never forget a medication ever again!

Recordsa all meds & creates times & units & u can produce a report at the end of month to give to GP/Specialist. 
Highly recommend & team that works on it, always helpful. Can get stuck & some conditions not included

To be Frank, I take so many supplements and medications. I was getting lost and not doing very well with my. Then my husband discovered this free pill reminder it’s totally free. It’s Brilliant I can’t live without it now.

titre: Good app but no dark mode
texte: No dark mode in app

Update:
Having had a problem with inventory update the developers have responded very well in looking into the issue. It is also noted that they also respond to comments in the Apple App Store - highly commended - would that all app developers were as proactive!
My wife and I have just started using the MyTherapy free app to replace “Medisafe” which has suddenly started to charge an exorbitant monthly fee for users taking more than two medications. MyTherapy is free and works flawlessly and is to be recommended. There is also a direct link to the website which also states “MyTherapy always has been and always will remain free to use.” One good feature that Medisafe has is the ability to customise the tablet image shown against the medication name by using a user selectable choice against shape, colour and score. Could this be considered as a feature for MyTherapy? It is certainly a helpful means of identification for those taking several medications.

This app is great for reminder alerts, especially if you take a lot of different medications at different times. The customisation is fantastic too, however the inventory count isn’t accurate. The app either double counts or doesn’t count at all meaning that the inventory number showing is wrong. Easily a 5 star app if they could fix this!

I have been using this app for a while now - it’s great as it reminds me when to take the various medications I have BUT the pill counter is inaccurate- it sometimes doesn’t deduct the pills you have taken and so the remaining pill amount is incorrect causing me to run out of meds- every few weeks I have to adjust them all so they are accurate - please fix this or I may need to find another more reliable app!

I’ve been using this for ages for cyclical HRT which is really difficult to remember and for no reason I can see the reminders stopped happening. I’d already missed several days medication before I noticed so I’m now looking for a backup app

titre: A total godsend if you struggle with memory
texte:
I would recommend this for anyone with complex medication needs and struggling to remember timings and what they have or haven’t taken.

Doesn’t have all medication but you can add bespoke items and track timings and inventory that way.

You can add different timings on alarms for week days and weekends so you can allow for lie-ins.
Great app for logging medication use, one offs, scheduled plus reminders for taking at set times. History can be reviewed and shared.

titre: Perfect for keeping track of multiple meds and reminding you to reorder when stock is low
texte: I take several medications that have to be taken at regular intervals. Before I used this app, sometimes I’d forget to take them, take more and then worry I’d overdone my meds. Also great for keeping track of how many tablets you have in stock and when to reorder.

This app has been a life saver. You can add as many meditations as you need without paying to upgrade. You can also get reminders for those meditations that you don’t take every day - it can cope with every eventuality. The customer service is really good too.

As title suggests, it's an okay app with limitations, I take a subcutaneous injection every two weeks and I cannot find an option to add this to the app. All my other meds seems to work fine. I'm using this in conjunction with Apple Health app and I'm gearing towards the Apple Health rather than this app. Sorry I think Apple wins this time around and I'll be deleting this app soon
Excellent

titre: Best free pill reminder out there
texte:
Used to use this years ago but moved to another app. And due to that app going paid I moved back to the best free pill reminder app I have used. 

I especially like how it has a widget showing meds and being able to mark them taken on my lock screen or Apple Watch makes it way to use. Definitely recommend.
This app is great - really helping me to remember essential medication. Great for people with ADHD!
An excellent and simple app which makes keeping track meds much easier.

titre: Medication reminder I would not do without
texte:
I need to take meds sometimes 3 or 4 times a day. I use this app (been using it for years as I write at the end of 2023). I set an alarm on my phone and also use this app’s alarm. This gives me two reminders one minute apart to make sure I don’t just ignore them. 

You can load every medication you take and the amounts and it will track when you take less or more or infrequent meds. I don’t really use it properly as I take so many different types at once. But it works really well for what I need it to do. I track my own doses and medical reviews etc in a spreadsheet as they do not change often.

Ideas for improvement - 
- have the alarm repeat every 10 mins. 
- alarm goes off when you switch your phone on even if you switch your phone on after the dose was required.

 It’s a great app and the support team are really good and responsive too. Big thanks for making such a helpful tool. 👍

Addition Dec 2024
I have a course of antibiotics to take. I typed in the name, a drop-down menu appeared, I chose the dose and how many times per day to take it and it incorporated it into my existing schedule. Genius! I was really impressed how easy it was! Well done team.
I need this so much to help me remember to take my meds and it has a nice musical note too! I would recommend it
Very visible, obvious alert on my phone. So useful - I’d not be without it and it’s free!
Great for doing what I need and reminds me to take my meds.

Pleased to have been recommended to me from a group. Had struggled with taking my meds on time. Sometimes even forgot to take them altogether. Now using this app, all that is in the past. The added bonus is the reminders for symptom checks. So grateful to the creator of the App. Thank you.
If you could add a function whereby you can add medications manually but are base and maybe add vitamin supplements then this would be fantastic and five star all the way!
Fantastic App! The only way to make sure you take your treatment!!
Really helpful!
I love this app! I’m terrible at remembering my meds (and feel terrible if I forget them)! This apps helps me stay on track and even keeps an inventory for me!
And free
Really helpful pill reminder with lots of options for setting up medications - it’s the first one I’ve seen with an every number of hours option too
easy to customise when you get the hang of it and to make changes, gives reminders. I wouldn't be able to remember or manage medications without it.
Easy to set up and use.. from going from zero daily medication to lots of different pills on different days this app has been brilliant!! So glad a friend recommended it to me.
Used this since I’ve been taking more meds and different appointments. Finding it really helps to remember everything

titre: Really not a fan of BMI aspect!
texte:
Have had to start test-driving other medication apps as my current one has decided to start charging a subscription. 
I like this app, it’s simple, easy to use, allows me to enter custom names as some meds aren’t recognised and I really like the fact that I can mark where I’ve put my morphine patch that week as this is something I normally have to write into the medication reminder as I have to rotate through multiple body areas over a 4 week cycle. 
My only irritations with the app are: 
1) I don’t like the idea of having to put my height and weight in nor my BMI measurement. BMI measurement is so outdated and multiple officials have called for it to stop being used.
2) My profile can’t be finished unless I give my height, weight & BMI and it can’t be more personalised with a photo etc instead of a person-shape photo. 
3) Lastly, please enable injection sites to be given for the back of the body - I wear my patch on my shoulder for 2 weeks out of 4. 

Have only downloaded it today so will continue to see how I fare with it.
Hi it notifies me, I open it, it freezes, it crashes, it goes white screen and crashes. There are better apps I’m sure! Strange how it starts to crash when I refuse to tip!!
Perfect pill reminder.
Great app, I’ve not got the greatest memory but this app gives me a little alarm to remind me, also it give you a nice picture everyday once I taken my tablets
The pill reminder really helps me as my condition comes and goes but I still need to take my medication.

This app was recommended to me by a nurse. It’s great as having tablets morning and evening sometimes I forgot to take them. Now I have the reminder and it gives you a record of your tablet taking which is helpful as sometimes you think did I take it and you can check. Better I think than the daily pill boxes.
Very helpful. Keeps me in tract with frequency and a whole lot more.
Very good app have used for quite a while now alarm for your medication very useful pill reminder also for liquid medication
Very helpful app I never forget my meds
Excellent app reminds me to take medication at recommend time. Easy to manage.
Useful, and it’s free. Though I’d prefer to be able to see a monthly view or longer, rather than only a week

I am awful with remembering to take my medications, and on top of that, I’m awful at remembering whether I have taken them or not. This app gives me a reminder at a suitable time for me to take my meds, and also I’ve set it up so that the notification is on my phone screen until I have confirmed I have taken them. This way, if I am in the middle of something when the alarm goes off, when I go to look at my screen after, I know I need to take them. This way I can also ensure I’m not double dosing. 

I have consistently been taking my meds again for the past year, and this app really has been a big help with this.
Reminds me to take my medication on time
Excellent app.

titre: A great app I don’t forget my tablets and drops
texte: This app has certainly helped me remember my tablets and eye drops. I rate it very highly. Just give it a try!

Highly recommended.

One of the best if not the best app to use with apple watch widget that sends sensitive reminders. Kudos to team for keeping it free and reasonably ad-free!

Used this app for a few years now, wouldn't be without it, so useful

I recently started using a tablet reminder app that I find competitive compared to other similar apps. It offers a broader range of features beyond just reminding me when to take my meds. The app also collects data on medication intake, which is useful for monitoring adherence.

I’ve used a few different apps and phone alarms to try and remember to take my medication over the years. This one has been terrific it works! I like that you can add different medications, with different durations and dosages plus appointments for different medical teams.

I have to take a lot of medication and my memory fails me sometimes so I need reminding. This app suits the job. I would recommend it to anyone who has a problem with their memory.

Really useful app. You can add different medications and have different levels of urgency. I usually remember to take mine but they are not daily so this app helps on those days where I do forget. I have been using this app for different medication for over two years now.

I take cyclical HRT & this app is the perfect reminder of which days to take the progesterone part of my medication.

There are many features available in this app and it is very intuitive. I personally use it to track times I take medication and it’s been a great help that I have come to rely on. I am never pestered by ads or anything. I would recommend this app based on my own experience with it 👍🏻

Edit: after extended use I have noticed occasions where push notifications are not sent which defeats the purpose of using the app for me. Also frequent prompts for donations and reviews lately.

titre: Simple and Easy Medication Reminder App
texte: I have been using this app for quite some time now and it’s a wonderfully simple and effective app. Absolutely recommended if you are looking for a medication reminder app that will just do the job.

titre: Great for keeping on track with HRT meds
texte:
I always struggled keeping on track with my HRT meds once I moved to oestrogen gel and progesterone tablets.

The oestrogen was easy enough as I need to take it every day, but the progesterone tablets are needed for 12 days starting on the 14th day of the cycle each month - so it got very confusing and I ended up taking the wrong dosage, either too much, or too little. This caused side effects that were uncomfortable but went away once I had finished the progesterone cycle.

I would set reminders on my phone but it was so hard to get right, then I found My Therapy App and it has helped me immensely! I think Dr’s should suggest this to all customers who a prescribed long-term medicines - it’s been a lifesaver for me!

Lots of accidents with which meds to take and when so we urgently had to find a solution for me. This app is brilliant at keeping count of which drugs you have (which you have to enter at the start) then you complete what you’re supposed to take and when. It then alerts you and it stays on your phone until you physically go into it and confirm you’ve taken the meds on your schedule! It will also remind you to re-order your prescription when you reach the minimum quantity you set up at the beginning. This app has been really helpful.

Easy to use and reliable reminder 
Has comprehensive inventory control too if you want it but I rarely use that
Highly recommended
It’s become too complicated to use with multiple “security” checks. Going back to fingerprint access would be a big improvement
Easy to use and to update you medication. Lovely alarm plays as a reminder. I would be lost without this app as I tend to forget to take my medication. Highly recommended.

Been using for a year and it’s so easy to forget to review the good apps and complain about the poor ones. This app is simple to use, it works and I use the free version and not had any issues. Out of four  medication reminder apps that I downloaded this is the one that stayed.
Invaluable for me who sometimes can forget to take my medicine

I missed my medication with alarming regularity until I found this app!
As the title says, Excellent!!
Really nice and helpful app
I was never sure if i took my pill before. More happier now. This medication reminder also allows me track my blood pressure. Great app, highly recommended!

Since using this app I have not missed a med! As someone on approx 7-10 meds at one time with carying doses and times, this has chsnged my life. It can be extremely confusing and time consuming even if you have a dosette box but this app really has been a game-changer for me! 100% recommend!

Ibw used this now for a few years > very helpful app. The ability to set reminders is most useful. The only thing i cant find is how to access records/report of meds taken & stopped & new drugs > although it might be in there but i cant find it
Amazing app.
Does what it says and more, pill reminders and simple to use

I’ve used MyTherapy for a few years to help me keep on top of what I’m taking and remembering when to take it! (I actually even have alerts set to remind me when to feed my cat! 😂🐈)

It’s the best medication reminder I’ve used and I really look forward to the daily pictures when I’ve completed my daily routine.
The only thing is, I wish there was a note to credit the photographer/artist and to let you know where some of the locations are.

Five stars and will continue to use for more years to come!
Kept forgetting my 2 weekly regimen until I found this app. Have medication twice weekly and 2 weeks per month this has been brilliant

This is such a great app for medication reminders. I struggled to remember if I’d taken my medication because it’s so automatic, this really helps. If I’m out and about it also really helps to keep me on track
Great app for reminding you and seeing your medication progress. The photos for completion are a great motivator too!

This is an app where I had absolutely no reservations making a donation to the developers - the app works really well. The downside is the alerts can’t be heard when the phone is silenced and so you would have to choose the ‘critical alerts’ option to get notifications.
It would be a good option to have a vibrate mode when the phone is in silent mode, just like other apps. Having the app make notification sounds during an appointment or class is not a cool option as most would like such things to remain private.
All in all a very good app and I look forward to further future improvements.
So easy to use and now I never forget my medication - absolute life saver!

Very useful app as a medication reminder. I often recommend to friends- it makes it so much easier to remember to take my inhaler and then because I tick it off on the app - I am certain that I have taken it. Also particularly useful for a medicine that I need to take every 3 days- easily forgotten otherwise because of the irregularity. I enjoy the daily reward pictures when all medication is taken- usually some cheering for the end of the day.
Couldn’t live without this app. Probably wouldn’t live if it were not for this app! Easy to use, clear reminders and warnings when pills run low. Excellent !

Great app. Really helpful medication reminder. I have Parkinson’s and have to take tablets every three hours. This is a great app to help with taking my medication on time throughout the day.

I love this medication reminder app, I find it  user friendly and never miss my medication now, I have recommended to my family and friends. The app developer is on hand to help too with any questions, great customer service.

I started to use Apple Health to track meds but it kept crashing - stopping the important notification reminders! I searched for an alternative and I found this - it is excellent. I love the Lock Screen widget, the support for critical notifications, the way it tracks pill supply, the lot. 

This is fabulous example of what apps should be: free to use but so well written and so reliable that you actually *want* to send the developers some money (which I have via their tip function). 

Thank you so much!

I have been using the app for a few months now to track my medication. Notifications and reminders make it easy to remember to take my medication and different times of the day and week days. 

I had a small issue with the app and contacted support I was very quickly contacted back by the great Jacek who was able to help with the minor issue. Definitely recommend this app for tracking all medication.

Have lost all my data and moved to a better app now.
Disgraceful

titre: So near but yet so very far
texte:
This is so close to being a great app, but unfortunately its failings and limitations let it down badly. 
I have found it very useful to help remind me when to take my various medications and set up is pretty straightforward. 
However, here are my main gripes with this app: 
1. It forgets to send notifications. A LOT. Every few days it forgets to ping me about a med so I miss that dose. That's not cool. And yes my phone is up to date and yes I'm running the latest version of the app. 
2. The red notification dot (numbered for multiple missed notifications) doesn't always show. This is particularly true in the all too frequent instances of point 1. 
3. You can't add a historical medication. I forgot to input a new med and although I could add it to the app a few days later it was frustrating that I wasn't able to adjust the start date to any earlier than that day. 
4. You can't adjust the snooze function. It's 30 minutes, like it or not. 
5. You can't undo an action. The other day my phone slipped in my hand whilst I was in the app and I inadvertently deleted one of my meds from the inventory. I was able to add it back in but I had to set it up from scratch and it's listed as a new item in the Progress tab, which is frustrating. 
6. If you take a med after midnight that is timed for before midnight (i.e. you're taking it on the same night but calendar-wise it's the next day) you can't change the date in the Progress tab. 
There may be other niggles but I can't think of them at the moment. 
Hope this is useful to someone.
The latest version no longer allows medication events to be marked as Confirmed or Skipped retrospectively. Please fix this ASAP.

I’ve only been using this app a few days, but seems perfect for me. Added my medication with ease. Notifications are loud enough so you don’t miss them, which I found with the Apple Health App Medication Reminders. You can add your total tablets left and get a reminder when running low and you need to reorder. Plus it’s free…..perfect! Thank you to the developers!

texte: I am really finding the app helpful and well designed. There is one tweak I would request - I can set meds to be taken on a cycle of x days active, y days inactive. Great for, say, contraceptive pill, where you might take for 21 days and then skip 7 days. Is it possible to invert it though, please? So x days inactive, y days active, for purposes of HRT? I am going slightly mad getting to turn my cycle dates inside out to fit the app structure! Thank you!

Made life so much easier, tells me what medication to take & when to take it and it keeps  an up to date inventory and warns when to re-order
I forgot things really easily and this app has made sure that I keep on track with my meds. Brilliant idea
App only allows use for items already in database. There is no functionality with regards to inputting medication not already in the database which pretty much renders the app useless for me.

It doesn’t work practically to ensure it supports you take your pills on time 

Developers I’ll write it here then update my review afterwards my email is currently playing up apologies 

If for example you normally take your pills every four hours, starting at 8am but you over sleep one day or wake up earlier and need your pills early there are no flexibility options for alert times to change to the new times for that day, (practically if you woke up in pain at 6am you wouldn’t sit there in pain and wait until 8am as is scheduled)  you would have to manually re change all of your setting for every pill for everyday ( which is not practical as there is bound to need to be a slight change a lot of days) Or all of the other pills that day you will be alerted at the wrong time and in essence it becomes pointless as you are still having to remember what time you actually had them. If there are many pills this also end is being very off for many days as to change them all would again be impractical 

Also there are no options if you take a lot of pills to have them alert at the same time ( I kind of like this as you can set a different time for 5 minutes afterwards as an extra reminder) but when you take ten pills plus the alarms are going off for a very long time when they all need to be taken at 8am for example, it does become slightly bonkers on the alarm front. 

Flexibility is needed for real life practical use 

Also I can’t work out a way to log oxygen saturation, just that you need to monitor it. I’m not sure if that’s just me or if it could be added that would be handy especially if that data could then be transferred to the graphs

titre: Was perfect, now important functions removed.
texte:
This was the most brilliant meds manager, and the one that got recommended to others. 
Really good function of the scanner, so as long as your meds are in the database, they can be added easily. Most aspects were perfect. 
Since a few updates, however, an important function has disappeared. The ability to mark a dose as “taken” from the notification has vanished for no reason. Now you have to open the app every time, which is totally unnecessary (especially if you’re at work or somewhere you don’t want to advertise what you’re doing). 
You can’t mark it as taken on the watch either, which was certainly possible before, and really good for marking it without drawing attention, or if the phone is not immediately accessible. 
Why such a useful thing has been removed is a total mystery, and needs to be fixed. 
This is the precise reason why this gets less than five stars, unlike my previous reviews.
I have missed the audible warning everytime, one ping, i feel a warning that has to be acknowledged would be better. Apart from that it does exactly what i want it to.

No graph details are available like in the advertised pictures either. It's a free app so I imagine my data is being sold on to the highest bidder. Wouldn't consider paying for it if the option became available based on current performance. Sorry folks.

titre: Made my life so much easier
texte: After breaking my ankle and having to have it pinned and plated back in November I’ve had to take a plethora of tablets since I came out of hospital. Until I found this app I was struggling to keep track of what to take and when. This app has literally made my life easier!
Great App to remind you to take medication . Works a treat

It’s really good to keep track of medication timings and your overall count of remaining pills. Some of the features are slightly clunky to use. And unfortunate the alarm only works if the phone isn’t on silent. Which mine always is. But nonetheless the best app to keep track.
Not sure how someone gave it a ( star when it is not recognising the bar scanner or the name of the tablet
Cannot fault this app, it’s simple, easy to use, and lets you track all the information you need!

titre: Great for remembering but minor ux changes needed
texte: Good but i wanted to update my medicine timer to mark the different time I took it (like an hour later) and it got confused, I didn’t understand how to do it. Other than that it works well and I definitely would’ve forgotten to take my antibiotics if it wasn’t for this.

Started off brilliantly but it doesn’t work now.
For whatever reason it doesn’t alarm any longer, which unfortunately is the idea. This obviously renders the app useless and, for now, will be deleted.

I appreciate the fact it’s free but to me t has to be because it’s a beta.  Pill counts don’t update properly. Syncing doesn’t work but hasn’t been removed.  Random strange bugs appear daily.  It’s just not reliable enough for me anymore.  I’ve reported a bunch of bugs and nothing seems to be fixed ever.

Recently had a hip replacement and was sent home with a fair amount of medication to take an various time’s of the day. With minimal setup this app allows you to organise your reminders with what medication is required at what time to avoid any double or missed doses.

Invaluable for anyone in a similar situation and highly recommended 👍
App worked well at first, but then it stopped sounding the reminder! No idea why, still shows on iPhone but without the tune to remind me it’s not much good anymore.

Whilst it’s a great app functionally, the alert sound rarely works and as a result I forget to take my tablets. My iPhone is set up as you recommend, and has ‘critical alerts’ enabled. Some advice would be good before I find a replacement!

titre: Great app really helps with reminding me to take my medication and control the quantity , excellent
texte: Excellent

titre: Works ok but has some problems
texte: Used this app for a couple years and always had problems with the audit count of remaining meds as despite taking all my medications on time every day, at the end of the course, it claimed I had more tablets left. For example, I would get 200 of one meds to last 25 days, yet after 25 days, the app said I still had 43 tablets left. Clearly not auditing correctly. Also, since the recent updates, when replenishing the tablet count every month, it made me do it three times for each med. Seems a bit odd that there is no save or update option for med auditing. Finally, for some reason when I put my email address into it, the amount of spam email I got overnight climbed four fold with health related emails. So going back to Medi-prompt as this app, in my experience, lacks accuracy.

Just discovered the app today, took minutes to set it up with all my different meds. Absolutely brilliant app, easy to use, simple to understand. 

Only thing I would like is when you order a prescription it allows you to put in the date you ordered it so you know you’ve done it.
Couldn’t live without this now. Great for timekeeping for pill taking. Can also keep a log of how many tablets I’ve taken and how many tablets I have left in my cupboard. Such a useful thing to have!
Very good app and very easy to use and it free 🙂
Really good for reminding about multiple medicines

I recently had a liver transplant and with all the immunosuppressant meds I have to take this app is an absolute godsend. Once configured correctly it works brilliantly & reliably. The only request I would have is the ability (via a tick box) to temporarily suspend a med. Otherwise perfect, very well thought out, easy to navigate & intuitive interface. Being a cloud based database, it can be accessed across multiple devices which is where similar apps are let down. I can’t praise it enough. If you need to take regular multiple medications this app really helps to keep you organised & on track.
Love this app. Now feel completely in charge of my meds and no nagging doubts about if and when I have taken my prescribed medicine.  Thoroughly recommended.
I’ve used this for a while now and it’s been great but the last 10 days or so it’s been very slow to load. Hopefully that will sort with the next update
I have used this app for a long time, but in the last few months it has started freezing whenever I try to change information.

Really easy to use. Nice interface. Gets the job done! 
Helped me get through my week’s prescription. Thank you.
I had this on my old Samsung and was delighted to find it in the iOS App Store. Simple to set up by scanning your meds bar code, set your reminders and off you go, no more missed meds.

This app is amazing and it should be adopted by surgeries. I’d like to join the team, make some recommendations and be an agent promoting this app to the doctors, and the nhs.

What a simply app that keeps things on track and alerts are great. 

Thx guys well done
Reminds me to take my pills
Using this to track complicated HRT schedule. Very effective and intuitive. Bravo.
simple, comprehensive and helpful

titre: Just what it says on the tin...
texte: A great pill reminder app. As someone with lupus, I take so many pills I rattle. This works well, is not over-complicated, but easy to tailor to my needs. As well — and this is a big deal for me — it’s not ANNOYING. With many such apps, they aspire to be the centre of your life, rather than just a help. I end up deleting narcissistic, needy, annoying apps😛. This does what it’s engineered to do; no more. Well done!
This app helps me remember to take my tablets, it is also good because if there is an emergency and I can’t tell people what I take this app tells them what I take and how much I have to take.
Easy to add medications and good customer support.

I wanted to monitor the effect on my health of the pills I take, and also be reminded of taking them each day. I can do this with this excellent app, I can write symptoms and get a report to analyse the results. It's great especially if you use lots of natural remedies and you want a simple way to keep track of their efficiency.

Really well done, just what I needed; the flexibility in my medication tracker to actually track what happens.

Most of the other apps in this field I couldn’t use because I was trying to track complex doses that they just couldn’t handle.

This one I could, and it’s pushed me to use the symptom tracker and nicotine tracker too to get a better picture of what’s going on.

The only thing I’d like if possible is the ability to track titration doses. E.g; “x pills y times a day, reducing by 1 pill every 7 days”. 

I know it’s not the most common, right now I’m just stopping x pills every day after 7 days and manually reducing the amount; so it’s the best tracker app I’ve found.

Loved the inventory feature; I genuinely always forget how many pills I have. Always.

Really well done guys, keep up the good work!

It was great to have the daily reminder for medication on my 8+. Non of the data transferred over to my new XR. Disappointed since nearly all of my data has transferred from backup. Tried to enter but app crashes every time.
Love this app

Scanning the barcode of my prescription failed. 
User interface for creating reminders is so poorly thought through. Utterly over complicated. 
You can’t even enter the number for how many times a day you should take your prescription. 
Seriously, this app had one job. I persevered in setting up the reminders as long as I could but ultimately just gave up.
This app has got to be the best on the App Store barcode scanner means it is very easy as I have a lot of tablets to take a big thanks to all

The app is useful and reliable, unless many apps I tried before. It reminds me to take my medication as well as to do my exercise at the right time. The settings have many functions and are very flexible to all kinds of schedules and medications.

Despite repeated efforts the alarm only worked ONE time in 20 attempts. On occasion I would get a notification instead of an alarm, but even that was sporadic. Such potential to be a great app with the ability to scan your own medication, but this fundamental flaw stops it from being worthwhile at all

As stated this crashes if I try and edit the medication. 

Also, seem to remember that confirming on Apple Watch would update on the iPhone app - this is no longer the case. 

Finally, it would be good if you could add additional doses. That is to say, normal daily dosage is, say, 2 tablets. But, you might take additional in case of a problem.

The app reminds me for a few days, then it will stop doing notifications and only show a badge once the app has been opened. I’ve deleted and reinstalled twice with the same result, works for a few days then stops.

This is a great little app that is very powerful in its functions, I simply wanted a pill reminder, however i have also been able to monitor and record numerous other readings for my GP including blood pressure & heart rate etc. 
The barcode scanner made it exceedingly simple and accurate to add my medication onto the system.
Great app, easy to use and always reminds me!!!

Set it up for my wife's anti sickness tablet which is every 8 hours starting at 8am ongoing (no end date).
Reminded at 4pm which was correct but not at midnight or ever again. Just says today is complete but I need it to carry on.
Bit confusing.

Addendum to this review.
Re-created the entry after deleting the original and, so far it has worked fine.
Can only assume it was a glitch.

After a minor stoke, my daily medication became much more complicated. I needed an app to help make sure I took the right pills at the right time. This app does exactly that; it's easy to use and free! 5 stars!

So important to keep an accurate log of medication as I for one and I imagine a lot if people have enough to worry just getting better.

This app allows me to do that.

This app really helps me to manage when to take tablets and supplements. I use it to record my health conditions, pain, etc It's very easy to use and provides reminders when to take medication and record how you are
This app was fine initially but now freezes almost like TSB buffering. Getting to the point where I may look at other options.
I loved this app but now it won't even open :(

Great App for me helping remind me when to take my medications and keeping track of what I've taken . It's simple at it works .

Just wish it would stop asking me to reauthenticate
This is a very good app. I use it every day and is very useful in keeping track of my treatments and medication.
I love the to do list for my meds! Great app! 5 stars!!! And first!

I really like this app. I like the Reminders. I like that it can remind you when to refill your medications. But I don’t like is for my evening doses. I take them, but I just forget to note them in the app the next day, you cannot go back in and update to say that you did take them. And also, I have been tracking my weight weekly and I cannot find anywhere in the app a graph a chart or anything to show me what my previous weights are so I’m just putting in my weight, not knowing what I was last time so I really think that could be improved other than that it’s a good app.

titre: Pop up adds ruined the ap
texte: They added ads and now when you complete a task (mark you have taken meds) it doesn’t show you’ve actually taken them and clear the alert.  You have to close the app once the ad pops up then go back in and reclick that you have taken the medication.   App was great before this but it’s annoying now.
Have been using this app for a couple years, and very much liking it. The new update is glitching and not letting me open it! Please fix!

I have 10 different meds to take weekly. My scheduling is very important as some of these press ent seizures. I have tried very many of these apps and this app provides me with all the info, ease and notifications that I require to keep my meds organized, safe and in control. 😅

I’ve been using this for many years for three things: Reminders to take meds, tracking consistency in taking meds, and tracking how many meds I have left.  This one will send notifications until there is an app update and then - consistently - it will stop sending notifications, which means I miss doses if I don’t remember.  I use this because I might forget, so it’s not very useful at doing the one job it has.  I’ve raised this to the support team years ago and it still fails after every update, and this app updates way too often.  I plan to switch over to another app as soon as I find one that works better.
It’s too bad there is t a phone call with a voice option. Or a way to customize your ringer on here.

I really liked the app until about three weeks ago, it just stopped sending notifications to remind me to take my meds (which is the only reason I downloaded it in the first place!) I’ve checked all the settings in the app and in my phone, but it still won’t send any notifications! Please fix this, it’s a great app otherwise!
A simple but effective and reliable app but lacks the option to repeat notifications if you haven’t marked medications as taken. Adding this feature would take this app to 4 or 5 stars.

titre: Не успела скачать, как уже полюбила это приложение
texte: Перешла из мэдисэйф потому что оно стало платным, и это гораздо больше платности заслуживает нежели то, и серия дней есть, меня это очень мотивирует не пропускать лекарство, спасибо за чудесное приложение, и бесплатное🫶🏿🫶🏿💞
Cross platform for Android and iOS. Has a pleasant yet persistent alarm so you don’t forget your med. I recommend it.

My husband has an Android & I have an iPhone and we both downloaded this app. His allows more functionality -why? He can set recurring reminders if pill not taken. Also he can select his own notification sound mp3.

titre: Good but no option for soundless notifications
texte: I like it but there is no option to turn off the sound for the reminders. Not even in my phones settings
Très utile pour suive et surtout ne pas oublier de le prendre
Bonne appli pour le suivi des symptômes
Très efficace pour ne pas oublier la prise de médicaments
The team helped  me change the logging time to “current” versus “scheduled” time.  I got clear instructions and had no trouble changing over.  This works much better for my lifestyle.

titre: There is always something not working for almost a year.
texte: I gave a try for almost a year. Wrote a few to the developers. Things are solved temporarily.  This app is useless if we don’t get notifications. The sound used to work but the red dot rarely appears. Now no sound no red dots even if the settings are right EVERYWHERE (I mean the app and the settings of the phone). Plus, even if I mark the meds taken it doesn’t save it so I have to remark the day after again and again and again and again. I’m so sick of this app. Not a single month in a whole year it didn’t have some problems. I keep it because I haven’t found anything else. How many updates will this need to work seamlessly for more than a month? How many times do you have to contact the developers? We are not talking about a game this is a medical help app! 🙄
App works well and nice inventory tracking. Works well with my watch. Full program not crippled. Pay if you use it!
Awesome app
Awesome app. Very accurate.
Excellent for daily check ups

I’m using this medicine tracker and one other to monitor my meds intake. By far this is the best. It is clear, easy to load information into and is constantly keeping track of the on-hand supply of each medication. Used in conjunction with a pill organizer, it is a perfect complement to regular medicine consumption. It has either audible or silent notifications. So, it is generally quite flexible for a variety of medication regimes. A tidy, elegant, very useable app that I wouldn’t be without.
I never forget my pills now. This medication tracker provides exactly the right amount of features without the bloat.
This app never lets me forget to take my medications where I was missing them before downloading it. I highly recommend this app!
I love this app. It is easier to configure and I have not missed any doses of my meds. It is the beat medication tracker I have used!

titre: Awesome App if your forgetful like me
texte: I take antidepressants and people can tell when I haven’t taken it for a few days. I am very forgetful and I was always worried of double dosing so I wouldn’t take my pills if I thought I’d taken them already. This app is saving my mental health.
Best medication tracker out there! Don’t bother wasting your time with others!
I always forgot to taken my vitamins on the right time. This app is perfect. I could organize all vitamins that I have and added different times to each one. Amazing!

titre: Sends medication reminders and lets me track them over time
texte: Helpful app. Allows me to track meds
Great app. I never miss my medication anymore.
This tracker has been very helpful as not only as a medication tracker but also for other medically related activities. It’s a good place to list my physicians and other medical providers.
The best app, thanks to the developers

J’utilise My therapy  depuis sa création et je ne m’en passerais plus.  L’équipe est toujours là pour répondre à nos questions, pour ajouter des fonctions que nous demandons ou pour corriger des petits bogues que nous leur signalons.

Bravo et merci à toute l’équipe de My therapy

I have been using “ My Therapy” for several years. Without their accurate medication reminders I would not be in the good place I am now. Forgetting just one daily drug sets me back so much. This app follows me throughout the day and keeps my drug needs on track. I thank you. Love the pictures too.
Great app for medication use. It keeps me organized and I can look back too.
This app helps me stay on track with my meds. It reminds me when to take them, which I love! The best part, it’s FREE!!!

Very happy with the latest round of improvements - there is just one thing left I would like to see - the ability to “snooze” an upcoming medication.  For instance - I was delayed in taking my med at 10am - which will mean I need to delay my 11am med too - rather than having to wait for the notification to go off, and then snooze it - I’d like to “pre-snooze” that notification.  Another scenario would be that I have a meeting and I don’t want the notification to be going off mid-meeting, so being able to delay the notification before I go into the meeting would be amazing!
Great medication and vitamin tracker. Very good. Well done devs
Love the App. I was missing my medication about 2 days a week. With this App I haven’t missed a day in over 3 months.
I find it works well for fixed times but not for my “take as needed” treatments

Got this app because most of the others ask wayyyyy too much information about you - this one doesn't. Thank you GDPR. It's a simple and basic tracker if you need a reminder to take a pill...or three. Alerts are loud but not annoying. It could have a few more features like configuration for future days, but for the "take your pills today" reminder needs, it does the job.
I use this app to keep track of my meds and it works well.
This has really helped me remember to take my meds and supplements.

I need to take meds when I first wake up - before eating. Since I'm usually waking up in a total daze, this app is great for reminding me to take them. It even allows you to enter how many pills etc. that you have left and reminds you when it's time to re-order.
I am completely dependent on this medication minder
Very useful for keeping track of meds.
Great app!   Love it!  Reminds me to take my pills otherwise I would forget!
This is the only free medication tracker app that does so well.❤️
Very good app. Very easy to use. Now  I can manage my medication very well.
It helps a lot, the ring tone is one of a kind😂💕💕
This app does it all.
Could not live without it
Useful versatile app lets you keep up with meds.

Recent update gives you bold large font.  Very easy to read. Reminders for meds, and inventory tracking. Can be configured to track half pills if required. Most meds are already in their data base making setup easy.
Found it very useful
Does a good job as a medication tracker.

Like many, I migrated to this app when MediSafe started charging an expensive monthly subscription. It was pretty easy to transfer everything and the interface is user friendly. Overall it is currently the best available medication tracker app.

The problems I’ve encountered are as follows:

1.  The inventory feature doesn’t always work correctly. I consistently log my meds, but the inventory often tells me I have more pills left than I actually do. This is a huge problem for me because I have memory issues. I now need to count my pills every day to make sure I refill them in time.
2. There is no advance reminder functionality for PRN medications. If you want to remember to take something like a pain or flu med 6 hours from now, you have to use another reminder app to do it. 
3. No option for multiple reminders for 1 medication. If I miss a notification, I don’t have the option of getting another one 10 minutes later or whatever.
excellent app for keeping track of medications
Very easy to use and make changes; the picture of the day is a really sweet little reward. It’s a good medication tracker - no complaints whatsoever.
Fun app to track time of meds
Use to make sure I don’t miss a Daily Pill. Love the little picts and inventory
Great medication tracker that is versatile and very helpful for keeping track of medications. Highly recommended! Top notch!!
Thanks so much keeping me focused
Its really good and has been helping me take my meds daily but I do wish there was more options to customize the app background, otherwise its good!!!

I try to like the app. I even donated to the team behind it. 
It does not sync between my iPad and iPhone and that’s a major problem.
It is good app though it needs addition of diary(note), full list of medication family doctor and consulrant name and contact number, if possible.
Love it except alert could be louder for those with hearing issues.  Also haven’t found where to edit meds when about taken gets changed ??  Otherwise good app

I tried this app in 2019, but it kept crashing and I sent a message for help but didn’t get any resolution, just that they would share this issue with the team. The “Symptom tracker” reminder crashes the app. No error message, just shuts down, showing the Home Screen of my phone. 
Tried again, new phone, updated app. Same problem! Sent another message but something sparked a memory and I looked to find the 2019 emails! 
No, thank you, moving on!
Edit to reply to Dev: I did receive a reply this time with instructions to log out, delete the app, reinstall, log back in…repeat 2-3 times and send a screen recording if issues persist. I needed a medication reminder app solution quickly, so I moved on. Perhaps I’ll try again in the future, but it was too frustrating.

I’ve used this app for years, even on my IPhone 15. Approximately 2 weeks ago, the app reset itself and now doesn’t let me set it back up again by crashing after entering the first medication even before programming specific reminders. I’m running iOS 17.6.1. I’ll upgrade my iOS tonight to iOS 18 and will revisit my review afterwards
I've used medication trackers before but this one is the only one that I've been able to stick with. Finishing my day with a nice picture reward is especially wholesome and I love it
Ever since using this app I have never forgotten to take me medication.  I used to forget very often, but not anymore.  It’s like a dream come through.

Hi Team,

Couple of suggestion in enhancing the user experience

In the inventory of the pills you can add as well how much needed for the treatment and the system can either calculate based on the duration and number of pills of how much needed or a manual input field,
Importance of this is that the system can give you a notification of when do you end this after three months six months etc. so based on the daily intake and how much needed he will be able to know.

Second is add another hyperlink to the details of each Supplements most of them are either both online or they have description of the medication online so a link would help.
Regards

I had an injury that required quite a few medications and I was forgetting to take my regular daily vitamins etc. This app was really helpful as each dose had different timing requirements. Highly recommend.
Seizure meds all taken on time for 3 years!
I love and swear by this app for 4 years. It’s helped me so much in terms of tracking my meditation. Love and recommend!!
Easy to use. Take the worry out of multiple medications and an old mind.

This app is perfect! Unlike other pill management apps, this one allows full customization of when to be reminded of refills and you can easily add additional prescriptions. I’m also enjoying the symptom tracker. 

This app is exactly what I was looking for. A way to quickly see how many pills I had, get reminded to refill at the custom amount I wanted and everything is done so smoothly. The colour theme is also quite nice and the photo reward is a cute idea.

Thank you very much, I am so happy that I can finally keep track of my medications.

I have been using it for reminder of my life saving medication for 5 years now. But for sometime now , not sure why but the reminder notification is not regular so moved to phone inbuilt medication reminder there in health.

titre: Easy to use and extremely helpful
texte: I always remember to take medication as the alarm goes off to remind me.
Love this app. I am so absent minded that I need it!!

I depend on this app to remind me to take my pills   And keep track of the meds as to when I need to refil my prescriptions   I am net very good at remembering my meds and often forgot to take them at all.
I started using my therapy a few years back. I have weekly injections and vitamins every other day. If I didn’t use this app I would be lost:
This is the app to keep you in line with your meds. I would be lost without it.  Reminds me when I have to take it and what I am taking. Reduces your stress of having to remember it. Two thumbs up!!
Great app. Been using it for a few years. Just keeps getting better. Recommended!
This app has saved me missing my medication doses countless times. Thank you
Makes it very easy to keep track
Love it. Very easy to use Highly recommended this to others

titre: I absolutely hate the new red logo
texte:
I’ve been using this app for a long time and it’s been a useful tool to help keep me medicated. 

The new red logo makes me hate the app. I don’t want angry alerts telling me to take my pills. I don’t need the extra anxiety from missing a dose with the big red alert on my phone.
Particularly helpful when starting new meds. Helps create the habit.

titre: Great pill counter and reminder app
texte: This is a great app, it helps me remember to take my medications, and helps me to remember if I’ve already taken them. It also tells me when I am due for a refill. Great app.
This app keeps me on my feet with my medications.
Love this app. Easy to setup and simple to use.

titre: This is the only app you need!
texte: I’ve been using this app for over a year now. I’ve also put this app on my mother’s phone. It’s reliable and keeps us on track daily! The pill counter is also useful and reminds me when I need to reorder my prescription.

Over the last 5 years I have used this app to keep track of my testosterone injections and it's fantastic! There's an option to track where you inject and I've not seen any other free app do that! Very easy to use, very easy to set up! I recommend it to all my trans friends!
Utile et très fiable. Je ne peux plus m'en passer

Living with severe nerve damage, means chronic pain which means meds ! And I am terrible at remembering, so when I found this app I was so stoked. I know…Lame, but trust me, sometimes it’s the small things in life that can bring you a little bit of joy lol 

I recommend this app to any of my fellow trigeminal neuralgia warriors that I speak too, especially newer ones that are just getting used to taking long term medications.

What can I say. This keeps track of medications and when to take them as well as when you need a refill. You can share this with your doctor and with family. You don’t have to carry all your medications yo appointments. It sends you messages when to take the medications and keeps bugging you. A true life saver thank you.
Great tracker, easy to use
I use it every day for medication reminders. It’s user friendly and the pictures are beautiful.
Great app. Only wish it had the ability to sync between different devices using the same OS
Been using this app for over a year for my meds and supplements and it works so well, has never let me down, now I never forget!
Great medication reminder and tracker
The best!

I have been much more consistent taking my meds and monitoring my blood pressure with this reminder, although the stored list of medication names seems to better align with the US than with Canada, where I am. The other features (e.g. “Support”) don’t work at all in my region.
As a person with adhd, having notifications to tell me when to take my meds is crucial. Also love the cute pictures you get when you mark the medication notification complete for that day!
I use this everyday, twice a day. No more forgetting whether or not I took my medicine. It’s a life saver!

I’ve been using your app for quite some time now. I’m logging my blood pressure as my doctor requires me to. But everytime I click on BP to check the daily BP records, it crashes and exits the app. Please fix this asap. My next check up is next week and I need to show my results to my doctor. If this isn’t fixed, I’ll have to look for another app to use. Thanks.
One of the better app. It does what it says, to begin with. It is written and presented in a clear language. It generates nice PDF. I would an export to Excel. Good work.

titre: Indispensable! I depend on it daily!
texte:
Totally reliable and user friendly. I have no problem even with medications that are not used daily, very easy to set schedules. 
Also provides medication time and inventory notifications! 
The best med scheduler out there!
This is the first app I’ve found that actually helps me remember my meds

titre: The best medication tracker app on the market!
texte:
I’ve been using this app for years, and it’s consistently gotten better. I don’t use any other med tracking app - just this. 

It’s so easy to use! It even creates monthly reports for you. It’s just that advanced!! 

Best part - it’s free!

I like this app a lot. It keeps me on track for my meds . I have Parkinson’s so I’m constantly taking pills. This app really helps me a great deal. Upgraded to 5* because the developer really wants user feedback and suggestions. Excellent app!

titre: Multiple meds on a fixed schedule 👍
texte:
I downloaded this medication tracker for my post-transplant medications that have to be on a fixed schedule. The alerts make it easy to take my meds on time. Being able to set reminder alerts for  getting refills helps as I need to use multiple pharmacies, especially as my doses can change mid refill. That I can track and get reminders for Clinic required statistics (ex: weight, blood pressure) is an added bonus. I was terrible at remembering these statistics.

I’ve had to change my country/region to find a couple of my meds but the app still lets me add them so a minor inconvenience. 

Would definitely recommend for folks tracking  multiple meds with or without a strict schedule.
I often get paranoid about accidentally missing a day of taking my prescription pills but this app gives me extra confirmation and reassurance if or when i think i’ve forgotten

titre: Apple Watch app would be perfect!

Does what it’s supposed to do - remind me when to take medication. Works simply and reliably. I like the picture at the end of the day - sometimes i use these as wallpapers.

While the support team and developers aren’t always able to incorporate feedback or suggestions into the app, they’re ALWAYS patient, gracious, timely with their responses, and helpful! Merci beaucoup! 😊

titre: Just updated it to useless app
texte:
It was a perfect app if i had 6-7 medications 3-4 times a day. As i can mark 1 medication as done and still remember which one i took. But now it marks all of them as done. And if i do it by mistake. I have to manually remember which one i already took. This upgrade made this app completely useless. 

And another scope for update is that when the midnight hits. Previous day’s medication goes to progress and disappears from Today. Now i have to go and mark each one of them individually and its a very long and complicated process. This is where we need mark all as confirmed from a day or a specific time.

There were a lot of problems with this app for almost a year. Consider that before picking an app that is so important for keeping track of your meds. It works well now but the fact that they took so long to fix the bugs is concerning.

texte: Not a secret that data here is shared by big Pharma otherwise would love to know more about your business model before handing over all our private health information!

titre: 10/10 the only medication tracking you need

this app has helped me a lot! i’m usually very forgetful but this has kept me on track

titre: Too many bugs and inefficient user flow.
texte:
Great that it’s free. 
Way too many bugs. E.g. randomly can’t track some medicines. A lot of other bugs.
The user interface isn’t intuitive. You need to do hit and trail to understand many buttons. Also the user flow is inefficient. Need to take unnecessarily long route to do some rather simple and frequent tasks. Recommend doing usability testing and user research.

It’d be perfect if cross-platform sync works more robustly, or if a refresh/sync button is added. Also, “as-needed” and scheduled medications are good distinctions. But, for things like supplements, sometimes I might want to take additional doses at a separate time. Say regularly, I schedule and take 1 fish oil capsule at noon. But if I add another 1 capsule at dinner, I would need to add it as an “as-needed” entry. Overtime, a lot of medication entries end up “as-needed” and duplicated. Thus, I would appreciate a choice of adding a medication intake entry and merge that entry into the “scheduled” medication in lieu of “as-needed”. I hope my description makes sense.

titre: It was good the way it was
texte: The recent update ruined the functionality of this app. Things are unintuitive and slow. You have to hit cancel to enter symptoms and hit done twice before an update will save. Responsiveness of the app is way slower and now we can’t enter custom symptoms. So disappointed.

It is very simple to use and does exactly what I need it to, help me keep track of my medications and remind me to take them. Love that I can check them off too, really helps so I don’t wonder if I took them or not! Would recommend
It really jealous me keep up with my meds, the alarm notification is nice. No complaints

Its a great tool and its very good for tracking whether I've taken my meds - but sometimes it randomly doesn't let me input info for a fay or two/stops giving me notifications/changes the inventory count/etc - which is frustrating, especially given that I rely on it a lot. 

tl;dr - great tool, a little glitchy but pros outweigh cons
This app has helped me so much keeping track of my antidepressants and my pain mads and all of that. 100% recommend!!!

This is truly a very helpful app to help keep track of a complicated medication schedule. It takes a while to set up the reminders, but once it’s configured, it’s great!

It would be extremely helpful if the developers could kindly add the following:

•• Unit of measurement: Dropperful

•• Method: Sublingual
    (And duration, 30 seconds, 1 minute)

•• Quantity/Amount: Fractions (like 1/2, 1/4)

Thank you. 😃
Finally on track with my medications.  Simple clean app that does what it is supposed to do!   Thanks a bunch!

titre: Works well for me, would recommend
texte:
Excellent app. I have been using it for 3 months and it works fine for me. When the reminder sounds, I sometimes put it off for “just 1 minute”, then can’t remember if I took my meds. But this app’s Confirm button has saved me on these occasions.

Also appreciate that the devs have made it available free, which is so helpful in these difficult times. Thank you!

I am often out of connection areas with data so I can still remind myself for my meds but have not found a way to update the app that I did in fact take the pills required on those dates. I wish that was available or if I’m missing how, please advise how to achieve the update to my progress. Thank you.

texte:
